confirmedHigh.title: onAnimationEnd exit-unmount is dead code; unmount always falls back to a hardcoded 300ms timer
confirmedHigh.detail: ENTER_MOTION/EXIT_MOTION are applied to the inner Toolbar (.action-bar__wrapper), but onAnimationEnd is on the outer .action-bar div, which has no animation of its own. handleAnimationEnd guards `if (e.target !== e.currentTarget) return;`, so the animationend bubbling up from the inner Toolbar (e.target=inner, e.currentTarget=outer) is always rejected and setIsPresent(false) is never called from the handler. Exit unmount therefore ALWAYS relies on the EXIT_FALLBACK_MS=300 timer, contradicting the comment that calls it a fallback for throttled/disabled animations.
confirmedHigh.evidence: index.tsx:55 `if (e.target !== e.currentTarget) return;` combined with className="action-bar" (outer, line 64) carrying onAnimationEnd (line 66) while ENTER/EXIT motion classes sit on action-bar__wrapper (line 71); action-bar.css has no `animation`/`@keyframes` on `.action-bar`.
confirmedHigh.suggestedFix: Either move the ENTER_MOTION/EXIT_MOTION classes onto the same outer .action-bar div that owns onAnimationEnd, or relax the guard to also accept animationend originating from the animated wrapper (e.g. check e.target matches the wrapper) so unmount tracks the real animation completion instead of a fixed 300ms constant.
confirmedHigh.verifyReason: The claim is verified by the code. ENTER_MOTION/EXIT_MOTION (index.tsx:18-20, tw-animate-css animate-in/animate-out) are applied only to the inner Toolbar via className="action-bar__wrapper ..." (line 71). onAnimationEnd={handleAnimationEnd} sits on the OUTER div className="action-bar" (lines 64,66). The outer .action-bar element has no animation or @keyframes — action-bar.css lines 7-15 define only layout (position/flex/z-index), and the file contains no animation/@keyframes at all. So the only animationend events come from the inner wrapper, where e.target=inner wrapper and e.currentTarget=outer div; handleAnimationEnd's guard `if (e.target !== e.currentTarget) return;` (line 55) always returns early, so setIsPresent(false) at line 56 is never reached from the handler. The exit useEffect (lines 43-50) sets setTimeout(() => setIsPresent(false), EXIT_FALLBACK_MS=300) (lines 23,48) as the sole working unmount path, contradicting the line-22 comment that frames it as a fallback for throttled/disabled animations. The test confirms this: the only unmount-after-close test (index.test.tsx:37-67) advances fake timers by 350ms to trigger unmount; there is no animationend-driven unmount test. Caveat on severity: the 300ms timer exceeds the 200ms animation duration, so the visible result is typically correct — the defect is dead code/wrong-mechanism rather than a user-visible broken animation, arguably medium, but the specific factual claim (onAnimationEnd path dead; unmount always via hardcoded 300ms) is exactly true.

confirmedHigh.title: Chevron expand button has hardcoded aria-label="Toggle" instead of localized Expand/Collapse
confirmedHigh.detail: The expand/collapse button at src/components/file-tree/index.tsx:256 hardcodes aria-label="Toggle". RAC's useTreeItem produces expandButtonProps with a localized, state-aware aria-label ('expand' when collapsed, 'collapse' when expanded) and injects it into the slot="chevron" Button via ButtonContext. But RAC's useContextProps merges via mergeProps(contextProps, props) (utils.mjs:100) where directly-passed props win for plain attributes, so the static "Toggle" overrides RAC's i18n label. The source comment on line 197 falsely claims the localized label is injected by RAC.
confirmedHigh.evidence: Line 256: `<Button slot="chevron" aria-label="Toggle" ...>`; RAC useTreeItem.mjs:30 sets `'aria-label': isExpanded ? stringFormatter.format('collapse') : stringFormatter.format('expand')`; the test file lines 82-83 explicitly flag the chevron a11y label issue as a deferred source-code defect.
confirmedHigh.suggestedFix: Remove the hardcoded `aria-label="Toggle"` from the chevron Button so RAC's slotted, localized expand/collapse label flows through; the onPress toggle still works because mergeProps chains event handlers.
confirmedHigh.verifyReason: The gap is real. The chevron toggle in src/components/file-tree/index.tsx:256 renders `<Button slot="chevron" aria-label="Toggle" className="file-tree__chevron">`. RAC's useTreeItem.mjs:30 produces a localized, state-aware label `'aria-label': isExpanded ? stringFormatter.format('collapse') : stringFormatter.format('expand')` (from @react-aria/tree strings), which is packaged into expandButtonProps (useTreeItem.mjs:33-45) and injected into ButtonContext as `slots.chevron = {...expandButtonProps, ref}` (Tree.mjs:610-612). But useContextProps merges via `mergeProps(contextProps, props)` (utils.mjs:100), and in react-aria's mergeProps later args win for plain attributes — so the directly-passed static `aria-label="Toggle"` (props) overrides RAC's context-supplied localized expand/collapse label (contextProps). Result: the chevron always reads "Toggle", not a localized/state-aware Expand/Collapse. The source comment at index.tsx:197 ("展开切换与本地化 aria-label 由 RAC 注入") is therefore false, and the test file index.test.tsx:80-83 explicitly documents this as a deferred source-code defect, noting "源码注释声称由 RAC 注入本地化 aria-label，但 slot=\"chevron\" 的 Tree 按钮 RAC 并不会自动注名" (the axe test only passes with aria-required-children disabled). The suggested fix (remove the hardcoded aria-label so the slotted localized label flows through; onPress still chains via mergeProps) is correct.

confirmedHigh.detail: The current-item contract token is rendered solely via data-current (a CSS hook) on the li and marker; nothing exposes the active item to assistive tech. A screen-reader user gets no signal which item is current.
confirmedHigh.evidence: index.tsx:122 sets data-current={isCurrent ? 'true' : undefined} and timeline.css:157 styles .timeline__marker[data-current=true]; the only ARIA in the whole file is aria-hidden on the connector (index.tsx:171) — grep for aria-current returns nothing.
confirmedHigh.suggestedFix: In the Item forwardRef set aria-current={isCurrent ? 'step' : undefined} (or 'true') on the li, so the current-item token is conveyed semantically, not just stylistically.
confirmedHigh.verifyReason: Confirmed by reading src/components/timeline/index.tsx in full. The Item forwardRef (lines 109-131) renders the li with only data-slot, data-status, data-align, and data-current={isCurrent ? 'true' : undefined} (line 122) — a CSS-only hook. The Marker (lines 146-166) likewise sets only data-current={item.isCurrent ? 'true' : undefined} (line 156). The isCurrent prop (line 32) flows into TimelineItemContext (line 116) and data attributes but is never mapped to any ARIA. The sole ARIA attribute in the entire file is aria-hidden="true" on the Connector (line 171). grep -rn 'aria-current' src/components/timeline/ returns nothing (exit 1). There is no underlying library, effect, or parent context that injects it — the component is plain React primitives. A consumer could pass aria-current through the {...rest} spread (line 124), but the component's own isCurrent API exposes nothing to assistive tech. There is no test file or demo in the timeline dir (only index.tsx) that overrides this. The suggested fix (aria-current={isCurrent ? 'step' : undefined} on the li) is correct and absent. Severity high/a11y is appropriate.

confirmedHigh.title: isAtBottom/scroll-button visibility never updates on viewport or content resize
confirmedHigh.detail: isAtBottom is recomputed only in handleScroll (onScroll) and inside the MutationObserver callback (DOM mutations). If the viewport height changes without a scroll or DOM mutation — window/container resize, or an async-loading image/iframe inside a message changing height after layout — the at-bottom computation goes stale, so the scroll-to-bottom button can stay hidden/shown incorrectly and stick-to-bottom can fail to follow. HeroUI's stick-to-bottom behavior tracks size changes.
confirmedHigh.evidence: src/components/chat-conversation/index.tsx: grep for ResizeObserver/'resize'/addEventListener returns nothing (exit 1). The only re-evaluators are handleScroll (line 120-122) and the MutationObserver (lines 152-166), which observes {childList, subtree, characterData} only — not element size.
confirmedHigh.suggestedFix: Add a ResizeObserver in the content effect observing both the viewport node and its inner content; on size change, run scrollToBottom('auto') when wasAtBottomRef.current is true, then updateAtBottom() — mirroring the MutationObserver branch so resize maintains stick-to-bottom and refreshes scroll-button visibility.
confirmedHigh.verifyReason: The gap is real and present in src/components/chat-conversation/index.tsx. computeAtBottom() (lines 90-97) depends on node.clientHeight and node.scrollHeight, but isAtBottom is only ever recomputed by three triggers, none of which fire on a size change: (1) handleScroll via onScroll (lines 120-122, wired at line 186) — scroll events only; (2) the MutationObserver (lines 152-166) which observes { childList: true, subtree: true, characterData: true } at line 166 — node/text mutations only, NOT element size; (3) the mount-only useLayoutEffect (lines 136-144). grep confirms no ResizeObserver, no 'resize'/addEventListener, no matchMedia anywhere in the file, and no parent/demo handles it. So a viewport/container resize, or an async-loading image/iframe changing scrollHeight after layout (no childList/characterData mutation, and attributes isn't even observed), leaves isAtBottom stale: the ScrollButton's data-state (line 413) and isDisabled (line 403) can stay wrong, and stick-to-bottom (followOutput, lines 156-160) fails to follow. The JSDoc on line 52 explicitly references the reference stick-to-bottom behavior, and line 277's comment shows the authors knew size-only changes don't trip their observer but treated it as a feature, missing the resize case. The suggested fix (add a ResizeObserver on the viewport + inner content, re-running scrollToBottom('auto') when wasAtBottomRef.current is true then updateAtBottom()) correctly mirrors the MutationObserver branch. Severity high is appropriate: stick-to-bottom and scroll-button correctness are core to this component.

confirmedHigh.title: Ripple does not fire on keyboard press (Space/Enter)
confirmedHigh.detail: The documented keyboard-press contract requires keyboard activation to produce press feedback. The Ripple's grow animation is started exclusively from a `pointerdown` PointerEvent (handleDown reads event.clientX/clientY), and the surface .--press/.--hover classes are toggled only by pointerenter/pointerleave/pointerdown/pointerup/pointercancel listeners (index.tsx:104-159). A keyboard user who focuses the button and presses Space/Enter gets RAC's data-pressed on the host but no ripple at all.
confirmedHigh.evidence: index.tsx:155-159 wires only host.addEventListener('pointerenter'|'pointerleave'|'pointerdown'|'pointerup'|'pointercancel'); handleDown at 104 depends on event.clientX/event.clientY so it cannot run without a pointer. No keydown/keyup handler exists in the file.
confirmedHigh.suggestedFix: In Ripple's effect, also listen for the host's keyboard press: on Space/Enter keydown (or by observing the host's data-pressed mutation), trigger a centered grow animation (startX/startY = rect center, equivalent to event at host center) and add .--press, removing it on keyup, mirroring the Highlight's data-pressed-driven behavior.
confirmedHigh.verifyReason: The gap is real. In src/components/pressable-feedback/index.tsx the Ripple effect registers ONLY pointer listeners (lines 155-159: pointerenter/pointerleave/pointerdown/pointerup/pointercancel); there are no keydown/keyup handlers anywhere (grep over the whole component dir returns none). The only code that adds the `.--press` class (line 107) and starts the WAAPI grow animation (lines 121-138) is handleDown, typed `(event: PointerEvent)` and reading event.clientX/event.clientY for startX/startY (lines 104, 115-116), so it cannot fire without a real pointer. The CSS that shows the pressed ripple (`.pressable-feedback__ripple-surface.--press:after`, pressable-feedback.css:123-124) keys off that JS-added `.--press` class, so a keyboard Space/Enter press yields no ripple at all. This contrasts with the Highlight layer, which is purely CSS-driven by `[data-pressed=true]>.pressable-feedback__highlight` (pressable-feedback.css:59-60) and therefore DOES respond to keyboard press because the root is a RAC Button (index.tsx:393) that sets data-pressed on Space/Enter — confirming the auditor's observation that the Ripple lacks the equivalent data-pressed driver. The keyboard-press contract is documented in docs/pro-parity-checklist.md:84. index.test.tsx only stubs Element.animate and asserts DOM presence; it never exercises keyboard press, so nothing refutes the gap. high severity is appropriate.

confirmedHigh.title: Undefined --shimmer-angle voids the shimmer gradient, leaving transparent text
confirmedHigh.detail: text-shimmer.css references var(--shimmer-angle) at lines 13, 18 and 94 with no definition anywhere and no var() fallback (grep found zero definitions and no @property registration). An unresolved custom property with no fallback is invalid at computed-value time, so the line-94 background:linear-gradient(...) declaration is dropped — but -webkit-text-fill-color:transparent and background-clip:text in the same block still apply, yielding invisible, non-shimmering text. This file is on the runtime path (src/styles/index.css imports lib.css, which @imports components/text-shimmer.css at line 149), so it does affect the live component, breaking the loading-animation and text-rendering contract tokens.
confirmedHigh.evidence: text-shimmer.css L94 `background: linear-gradient(calc(90deg + var(--shimmer-angle)), ...)` plus L95 `-webkit-text-fill-color: transparent;` / L99 `background-clip: text;`, with `grep -rn shimmer-angle` showing only the 3 usages and no definition/@property.
confirmedHigh.suggestedFix: In src/components/text-shimmer/index.tsx, provide a default --shimmer-angle (e.g. 0deg) and the other unset inputs (--shimmer-track-width) via an inline style on the span so the gradient is valid regardless of the missing CSS var, e.g. style={{ ['--shimmer-angle']: '0deg', ...style }}.
confirmedHigh.verifyReason: Verified the gap is real. (1) src/components/text-shimmer/index.tsx renders a bare span with no inline style — no default for --shimmer-angle (lines 6-10). (2) src/styles/components/text-shimmer.css uses var(--shimmer-angle) with NO fallback at L13 (`tan(var(--shimmer-angle))`), L18, and L94 (`linear-gradient(calc(90deg + var(--shimmer-angle)), ...)`), yet never defines it; the .text-shimmer rule (L7-25) defines --shimmer-track-height, --_speed etc. but not --shimmer-angle. (3) No @property registration: src/styles/properties.css (96 @property lines) has no --shimmer-angle. (4) No external/token/inline definition anywhere — grep across src and dist found the 3 undefined usages and nothing else; demos in ai-overlay-demos.tsx only set `color` (L693-695), never --shimmer-angle. (5) Runtime path confirmed: src/main.tsx imports src/styles/index.css → `@import './lib.css'` (index.css L12) → `@import './components/text-shimmer.css'` (lib.css L149); same code is in built dist/styles.css L28676/28681/28757. Per CSS spec, var() with no fallback resolving to a missing property yields the guaranteed-invalid value, making the L94 `background` declaration invalid-at-computed-value-time (IACVT) so it resets to transparent/no-image, while L95 `-webkit-text-fill-color: transparent` and L98-99 `background-clip:text` in the same block still apply → invisible, non-shimmering glyphs. Only deviation from the claim: the @import is at lib.css L149, not index.css (index.css imports lib.css) — a harmless citation slip; the substance holds. Note: the prefers-reduced-motion block (L108-116) restores visible text for reduced-motion users, so the breakage hits the default animated path — confirming, not mitigating, the gap. confirmedHigh.title: No keyboard drag-and-drop for reordering or moving cards
confirmedHigh.detail: Motion's Reorder/useDragControls is pointer-only. The DragHandle wires only onPointerDown (it calls dragControls.start(event) from a ReactPointerEvent), with no onKeyDown/keyboard pick-up-and-move path, so cards cannot be reordered within a column or moved across columns via the keyboard.

DragHandle (lines 473-503) wires ONLY onPointerDown -> dragControls.start(event) (handler at 477-488); it has no onKeyDown/onKeyUp, no Space/Enter grab, no Arrow move, no Escape cancel, and no aria-roledescription. confirmedHigh.title: Attachment drag-and-drop drop zone is entirely absent

confirmedHigh.verifyReason: The gap is real. In src/components/rich-text-editor/index.tsx:655-666, BubbleMenu's only logic is `data-visible={editor.isFocused ? 'true' : undefined}`; it never reads selection or positions itself. A selection range IS captured in lastSelectionRef (line 283, 302-303 clones selection.getRangeAt(0)), but it is consumed only by restoreSelection (327-336) during execCommand-based runCommand/setLink/unsetLink — never by BubbleMenu. The CSS (src/styles/components/rich-text-editor.css:198-217) reinforces this: .rich-text-editor__bubble-menu uses margin-inline:auto (statically centered, no absolute positioning), and the [data-visible=true] rule only toggles opacity/pointer-events/transform. So the menu appears on any focus — including a collapsed caret — and is not anchored to the selection rect. The auditor's secondary claim that "grep for positioning returned nothing" is actually accurate. confirmedHigh.title: MenuToggle is a toggle button, not a disclosure: no aria-expanded / aria-controls to the Menu
confirmedHigh.detail: The mobile MenuToggle renders a RAC ToggleButton (index.tsx:431-438) which emits only aria-pressed (confirmed by test index.test.tsx:81). MenuToggle (src/components/navbar/index.tsx:430-443) renders a RAC ToggleButton setting only aria-label, isSelected, onChange, plus {...rest}; it never wires aria-expanded or aria-controls. The Menu div (index.tsx:492-495) has data-slot="navbar-menu" but no id, so there is no target an aria-controls could point to; grep finds zero aria-expanded/aria-controls/id/useId in the whole navbar component. The test (index.test.tsx:81) asserts only aria-pressed="true", and the demo (src/showcase/demos/feedback-nav-demos.tsx:639-647) renders Navbar.MenuToggle and Navbar.Menu wired purely through context state with no ARIA relationship. confirmedHigh.title: Search filter cannot match emoji by name — only matches raw glyph or category label

The empty-state test (lines 86-95) even relies on 'zzzznomatch' producing the empty state, never asserting a real keyword match.

confirmedHigh.verifyReason: matchesSearch (index.tsx:92-97) is `emoji.includes(q) || category.label.toLowerCase().includes(q) || category.id.includes(q)`. `emoji` is a raw glyph (data model EmojiCategory.emojis: string[], lines 28-34) with no name/keyword field, so `emoji.includes('smile')` is always false. category.label values are Chinese ('表情','手势','动物','食物','物件', lines 64/70/76/82/88) so English keywords never match labels. Only category.id is English (lines 61-89), so 'animals'/'food' match by id substring, but 'heart'/'dog'/'cat'/'smile' (as a name) return the empty state. The code's own comment (line 93) admits emoji carry no readable text and matching is label-based, only "enough to demo real-time filtering". The empty-state test (test lines 86-95) types 'zzzznomatch' and asserts only the empty state — no test ever asserts a real keyword like 'smile' surfaces the smiley emoji. Genuine per-emoji name/keyword search (HeroUI Pro's behavior) is structurally absent. Gap is real.

confirmedHigh.title: Drag-end snaps only ±1 step, never to the nearest snap point
confirmedHigh.detail: HeroUI snaps to the nearest configured point on release. handleDragEnd only ever moves one step (currentSnapPoint-1 or +1) regardless of how far the user dragged, so a long drag across multiple snap points lands on the adjacent one instead of the closest.
confirmedHigh.evidence: index.tsx:359-375: only `setSnapPoint(currentSnapPoint - 1)` / `setSnapPoint(currentSnapPoint + 1)` are called, gated on a single snapDistance threshold; there is no computation of the resulting position vs. all snap points to pick the nearest.
confirmedHigh.suggestedFix: In handleDragEnd, compute the projected position from current size + directed travel (and velocity) and select the nearest entry in resolvedSnapPoints, allowing multi-step jumps.
confirmedHigh.verifyReason: handleDragEnd (src/components/sheet/index.tsx:345-384) only ever moves one index. Lines 360-366 call setSnapPoint(currentSnapPoint - 1) and lines 368-374 call setSnapPoint(currentSnapPoint + 1), each gated solely on a single binary threshold: directedTravel > snapDistance OR directedSpeed > VELOCITY_THRESHOLD. snapDistance is one clamped value (lines 354-357: min 48 / max 120px). There is no computation of the projected release position (current size +/- travel, optionally + velocity) against resolvedSnapPoints to find the nearest entry — no loop or distance comparison exists anywhere in the file. The only other snap-related code is snapStyle (lines 303-310) which just renders the size of the current index, and motion's dragSnapToOrigin (line 401) which merely resets the drag transform to 0; neither performs nearest-point selection. Concrete failure with the demo's snapPoints [220,340,480] (ai-overlay-demos.tsx:2771) starting at index 2 (480px): a fast 230px downward drag projects to ~250px (nearest = index 0 / 220px), but the code matches directedTravel > snapDistance (230 > 120) with currentSnapPoint > 0 and executes setSnapPoint(2-1)=index 1 (340px), then returns — landing on the adjacent point, never the nearest. The test file (index.test.tsx) does not exercise snap behavior at all (jsdom has no layout), so nothing covers this. The cited lines and the gap are accurate.

confirmedHigh.title: Trigger has no ARIA relationship to the overlay (no aria-expanded / aria-describedby)

confirmedHigh.title: Copy icon swap is static, not the animated HeroUI transition

confirmedHigh.title: Cross-column drop ignores drop position within the target column
confirmedHigh.detail: useKanbanColumn.onDragEnd only resolves WHICH column the pointer landed in and calls kanban.moveItem, which routes through update() — replacing the card in place in the global array. The card therefore keeps its original array slot/relative position instead of being inserted at the pointer's drop location in the target column.
confirmedHigh.evidence: src/components/kanban/index.tsx:779-781 (`kanban.moveItem(getKey(item), targetColumn)`) -> moveItem (680-688) -> update (615-619) maps in place; onDragEnd computes only targetColumn, never a target index or before/after sibling.
confirmedHigh.suggestedFix: In onDragEnd, after detecting the target column, hit-test the dragged point against that column's card rects to find the insertion index, then move the item to that exact position (setColumn + moveBefore/moveAfter) so cross-column drops land where the user dropped.
confirmedHigh.verifyReason: The gap is real. In src/components/kanban/index.tsx, useKanbanColumn.onDragEnd (lines 752-784) only hit-tests the pointer against COLUMN list rects to resolve targetColumn — `const rect = listEl.getBoundingClientRect()` on `[data-slot="kanban-card-list"]` elements (766-767). It never hit-tests against individual card rects, computes an insertion index, or calls moveBefore/moveAfter. The only cross-column action is `kanban.moveItem(getKey(item), targetColumn)` (line 780). moveItem (680-688) calls `update(key, setColumn(item, toColumn))`, and update (615-619) is `prev.map((item) => keyOf(item) === key ? next : item)` — an in-place value swap that preserves the card's original global array slot. Since useKanbanColumn.items filters list.items in array order (738-741), the moved card surfaces in the target column wherever its old slot lands, not at the drop point. moveBefore/moveAfter (641-648) and reorderColumn (651-673) exist but are wired only to onReorder for same-column reordering (743-748), never to cross-column drops. Cited line numbers all check out (claimed 779-781 vs actual moveItem call at 780; moveItem 680-688; update 615-619). The test file even notes cross-column hit detection is untestable in jsdom (index.test.tsx:6), so no coverage refutes this. Severity high is appropriate — cross-column drop positioning is a core kanban interaction; only same-column ordering works correctly (via Motion's live onReorder).

confirmedHigh.title: Streaming variant does not memoize blocks; every token re-renders all blocks
confirmedHigh.detail: HeroUI Pro's Streaming variant uses memoized blocks so a token update only re-renders changed content. Vela re-parses the entire source on each render and keys blocks by positional index, with no React.memo, so appending one token re-renders and re-mounts-risk every block.
confirmedHigh.evidence: parseBlocks runs over the full source each render (index.tsx:522) and blocks are keyed by `${seed}-${index}` positional index (index.tsx:529); no memo()/React.memo anywhere in the file (grep for memo( returned nothing).
confirmedHigh.suggestedFix: Wrap each rendered block in a memoized component keyed by stable block content (e.g. hash of segment source) so unchanged blocks skip re-render during streaming, matching the documented memoized-block behavior.
confirmedHigh.verifyReason: Read full src/components/markdown/index.tsx. Confirmed all three assertions. (1) Line 522: `const blocks = useMemo(() => parseBlocks(children, { components }), [children, components]);` — parseBlocks (line 495) runs over the FULL source via parseSegments (line 430, splits entire source). It is wrapped in useMemo, but keyed on `children`, so every streamed token mutates `children` and forces a full re-parse — the memo gives zero benefit during streaming. (2) Lines 526-529: blocks are keyed by positional array index `key={`${seed}-${index}`}`, with an eslint-disable comment explicitly acknowledging index keys; not keyed by content. (3) No component-level memoization: each block's `node` is a freshly-created ReactNode rebuilt inside parseBlocks on every render, and there is no React.memo wrapping rendered blocks (grep found only useMemo at lines 6/521/522). CodeBlock (the fenced-code renderer) also has no memo. So appending one token re-parses and re-renders every block — exactly the gap claimed. Minor imprecision in the auditor's note ("grep for memo( returned nothing"): useMemo does exist, but it is source-keyed and does not provide per-block memoization, so the substantive gap is real. No test in index.test.tsx covers render-skipping behavior. Severity 'high' is reasonable for streaming-AI scenarios but arguably medium since correctness is unaffected (functional output is correct; this is a re-render/perf concern only) — keeping as-is is defensible.

confirmedHigh.title: Snap drag is discrete, not continuous — panel does not follow the pointer toward the next snap point
confirmedHigh.detail: HeroUI Pro snap sheets continuously update the panel size during drag (docs: "--snap-point-height is updated via JavaScript during interaction") and snap to the NEAREST point on release. Here the panel's height/width is fixed by snapStyle while dragging; only the surface's translate (motion y/x offset) moves, and height only changes discretely after release via a CSS transition.
confirmedHigh.evidence: index.tsx:393-419 the motion.div translates via `y: offset`/`x: offset` with `dragSnapToOrigin`, while the Drawer.Dialog size comes from `snapStyle` (height/width) at index.tsx:303-310,431; there is no `onDrag` handler updating any `--snap-point-height` and no continuous size interpolation.
confirmedHigh.suggestedFix: Add an `onDrag` handler that maps live drag offset to an interpolated size (or a CSS var consumed by the dialog) so the panel grows/shrinks while dragging, matching vaul/HeroUI continuous follow.
confirmedHigh.verifyReason:
Confirmed by reading src/components/sheet/index.tsx and src/styles/components/sheet.css. During a snap drag the panel does NOT continuously resize toward the next snap point — it only translates as a rigid block, and the size jumps discretely on release.

Mechanics traced exactly:
- The live drag motion value `offset` (index.tsx:315) is consumed ONLY as `y: offset` / `x: offset` on the motion.div (index.tsx:416-417) — a pure CSS transform translate. grep confirms `offset` is never piped into height/width via useTransform or a CSS var.
- The Drawer.Dialog's size comes from `snapStyle` (index.tsx:305-310, applied at :431), derived from `currentSnapSize` -> `currentSnapValue` -> `currentSnapPoint` (a discrete index, :296-304). That index does not change during drag.
- The motion.div has only `onDragStart`/`onDragEnd` (index.tsx:420-421) — there is NO `onDrag` handler. `setSnapPoint` is called only from `cycleSnapPoint` (:342) and inside `handleDragEnd` (:364, :372) — never mid-drag.
- On release, `dragSnapToOrigin` (index.tsx:401) springs the translate offset back to 0 while the new index flips the size, which then animates discretely via CSS `transition: height .24s ..., width .24s ...` (sheet.css:165).
- There is no `--snap-point-height` (or any) CSS variable updated during interaction; grep of styles/ finds none.

Net: the panel follows the pointer via translate only, keeps a fixed intrinsic size during drag, and changes size discretely (translate snaps to origin + CSS size transition) after release — matching the auditor's description. Note the snap-to-nearest-on-release end state IS implemented correctly; the gap is purely the lack of continuous size interpolation during drag, so this is a feel/animation-fidelity defect rather than broken functionality.

confirmedHigh.evidence: index.tsx has no sourceType prop; both root and Preview hardcode `href`, `target="_blank"`, `rel="noopener noreferrer"` (lines 104-110, 164-172). ai-overlay-demos.tsx lines 2079-2086 implement variant==='document' with <ChatSource.Preview href="https://github.com/.../README.md" /> — a normal link.
confirmedHigh.suggestedFix: Add a `sourceType?: 'url' | 'document'` prop; for 'document' render a non-anchor card (span/button) with a document icon and no target=_blank navigation, matching HeroUI's document indicator.
confirmedHigh.verifyReason: The gap is real. index.tsx exposes no sourceType prop: ChatSourceProps (lines 27-32) and ChatSourcePreviewProps (lines 34-40) only declare href/title/iconSrc/fallback/description plus open-feedback props. Both render paths hardcode an external-link anchor: ChatSourceRoot renders `<a href={href} target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">` (lines 104-108) and Preview renders the same (lines 164-169). grep across the component finds zero `document`/`sourceType` references in the TSX. There is no conditional non-anchor branch anywhere. The demo's `document` variant (ai-overlay-demos.tsx lines 2079-2086) is just `<ChatSource.Preview href="https://github.com/HollyCci/vela-ui/blob/main/README.md" ... />`, i.e. a normal target=_blank link, not a document indicator. The only trace of a document affordance is an orphaned `.chat-source__document-icon` CSS rule (src/styles/components/chat-source.css line 58) that is never referenced by any element in the component (grep confirms no usage in src/), and per project convention those component CSS fragments are detached/non-loading anyway. So HeroUI's document-type source (non-link document indicator) is genuinely absent.

confirmedHigh.title: animated-change contract is completely unimplemented
confirmedHigh.detail: The audit lists `animated-change` as a documented contract token, but src/components/number-value/index.tsx renders the formatted string directly with no state, no useEffect, no Motion, and no CSS transition hook — when `value` changes the text swaps instantly with zero animation (count-up/roll/cross-fade).
confirmedHigh.evidence: index.tsx:27-29 renders `{new Intl.NumberFormat(locale, formatOptions).format(value)}` inline; grep for useState/useEffect/useRef/motion/transition/animate/requestAnimationFrame in index.tsx returns nothing (exit 1).
confirmedHigh.suggestedFix: Track the previous value via useRef/useState and animate the transition on value change (e.g. a Motion/Framer count-up or interpolated tween that re-formats intermediate values through the same Intl.NumberFormat), ideally gated by prefers-reduced-motion.
confirmedHigh.verifyReason: The gap is real. src/components/number-value/index.tsx (full file, 42 lines) is a stateless forwardRef that at lines 27-29 renders `{new Intl.NumberFormat(locale, formatOptions).format(value)}` directly into a span — no useState/useEffect/useRef/Motion/rAF anywhere; value changes swap the text instantly with no animation. The only stylesheet, src/styles/components/number-value.css, is 10 lines total and contains just `font-variant-numeric: tabular-nums` (line 8) — no transition, animation, or @keyframes hook. index.test.tsx covers only formatting/prefix/suffix slots and axe, with zero animation tests. No parent context or underlying library supplies the behavior; the component is a plain inline span. Meanwhile `animated-change` is an explicitly documented contract token (scripts/pro-parity-contract.cjs:51: 'number-value': ['number-formatting', 'animated-change', 'locale-options']) and the checklist (docs/pro-parity-checklist.md:83) itself admits it "still needs animated-change." So the documented contract token is genuinely unimplemented.

confirmedHigh.title: custom-value contract is faked at the demo level — no custom Select.Value render
confirmedHigh.detail: HeroUI Pro's 'Custom value' variant renders a customized representation of the selected option via a Select.Value function/render-prop child. The Vela `cell-select-custom-value` demo never does this; it only interpolates `slotLabel` into the outer Trigger Label text while CellSelect.Value still renders the plain default. The contract behavior is thus demonstrated by string concatenation, not by the component's value-rendering API.
confirmedHigh.evidence: forms-demos.tsx:1236-1243 `if (variant === 'custom-value') { return ...renderSlotSelect(`当前时段：${slotLabel}`, slot)... }` — and renderSlotSelect (line 1165) emits a bare `<CellSelect.Value />` with no function child anywhere in the file.
confirmedHigh.suggestedFix: Add a demo that passes a render-prop/function child to CellSelect.Value (e.g. `<CellSelect.Value>{({selectedItem,isPlaceholder})=>...}</CellSelect.Value>`) to exercise SelectValueRenderProps, and add a test asserting the custom-rendered value markup appears in the trigger.
confirmedHigh.verifyReason:
The demo-level gap is real. The `custom-value` variant at src/showcase/demos/forms-demos.tsx:1236-1243 does `renderSlotSelect(`当前时段：${slotLabel}`, slot)`, and renderSlotSelect (around forms-demos.tsx:1165) emits a bare `<CellSelect.Value />` (line ~1165) with no function child. The "custom value" is produced by interpolating `slotLabel` into the `<CellSelect.Label>` text, not by Select.Value's render-prop API. I grepped the whole file path and found no `<CellSelect.Value>{...}` function child or use of selectedItem/isPlaceholder anywhere; the test file (src/components/cell-select/index.test.tsx) likewise only uses bare `<CellSelect.Value />` (line 18) and has zero coverage of SelectValueRenderProps. So HeroUI Pro's "Custom value" render-prop pattern is demonstrated by string concatenation, exactly as claimed.

HOWEVER the severity is overstated as "high". The component itself fully supports the contract: CellSelect.Value (src/components/cell-select/index.tsx:127-133) is a thin wrapper that spreads `...rest` into Select.Value, and the underlying react-aria SelectValueProps extends `RenderProps<SelectValueRenderProps<T>, 'span'>` (node_modules/react-aria-components/dist/types/src/Select.d.ts:86-94, with selectedItem/isPlaceholder/selectedText/state at lines 66-85). A consumer can pass `<CellSelect.Value>{({selectedItem,isPlaceholder})=>...}</CellSelect.Value>` today and it works — nothing is faked or broken at the API level. The gap is purely demo/test fidelity (the Pro "Custom value" variant isn't actually exercised), not a missing capability. Adjusted to medium.

confirmedHigh.title: maxLength is displayed but never enforced
confirmedHigh.detail: The contract token character-count implies a limit; the prop is plumbed only into CharacterCount display and the data-over-limit flag. handleInput/syncFromDom/runCommand never check maxLength, so typing, paste, and commands can grow content unbounded past the limit.
confirmedHigh.evidence: src/components/rich-text-editor/index.tsx:708-713 maxLength only drives the count label and `data-over-limit`; grep shows no maxLength reference inside emitChange/handleInput/runCommand.
confirmedHigh.suggestedFix: In syncFromDom/handleInput, compare the new text length against maxLength and revert the DOM (or trim) when exceeded, mirroring a real editor's hard limit, while keeping the over-limit visual state for soft limits if desired.
confirmedHigh.verifyReason: maxLength is plumbed only into display. grep across the component shows it referenced at index.tsx:60/108 (types), :270 (destructure), :389/406 (context value + useMemo dep), and :702/708/713 inside CharacterCount where it drives the `data-over-limit` attribute and the `/ max` label — nowhere else. All mutation paths lack any length guard: emitChange (285-296) computes characterCount but never clamps; syncFromDom (338-343) emits element.innerHTML unconditionally; runCommand/setLink/unsetLink (345-371) call execCommand then syncFromDom with no check; Content.handleInput (613-616) and the Tab-insert in handleKeyDown (618-626) call editor.handleInput() (=syncFromDom) with no length comparison or DOM revert. There is no onPaste/beforeinput interception and no truncation. So typing, paste, and commands can grow content past maxLength; the only consequence is the visual over-limit flag. Claim is accurate. Severity note: arguably medium not high — the count label + data-over-limit make this a defensible soft-limit UI, though the `maxLength` name implies hard enforcement and no separate soft-limit affordance exists.

confirmedHigh.title: event-delete has no pointer affordance (keyboard-only)
confirmedHigh.detail: Deletion only fires from a keydown of Delete/Backspace on a focused event. There is no click/right-click/popover/menu/delete-button path, so a mouse- or touch-only user can never delete an event, which the event-delete contract implies.
confirmedHigh.evidence: In every event variant (Event L1526-1531, AllDayEvent L964-969, MonthEvent L1871-1876, MonthSpanningEvent L1813-1818) onEventDelete is invoked exclusively inside handleKeyDown on key==='Delete'||'Backspace'; grep for Popover/menu/aria-haspopup in index.tsx returns nothing.
confirmedHigh.suggestedFix: Open a small event-detail Popover (or context menu) on event click in index.tsx that contains a real Delete button calling onEventDelete(event.id), so deletion is reachable by pointer as well as keyboard.
confirmedHigh.verifyReason: All four event variants route pointer clicks only to selection, never deletion. handleClick bodies do `setSelectedEventId(event.id)` exclusively: AllDayEvent index.tsx:954-957, Event :1516, MonthSpanningEvent :1803-1806, MonthEvent :1861-1864. onEventDelete?.(event.id) fires ONLY inside handleKeyDown guarded by key==='Delete'||'Backspace' (lines 966-969, 1528-1531, 1815-1818, 1873-1876). grep for popover|contextmenu|aria-haspopup|menu|trash|delete-button|right-click in index.tsx returns nothing (the only `remove*` hits are removeEventListener drag cleanup at 1192-1194/1490-1492); no onContextMenu handler exists. The "more"/date/today buttons (e.g. 1770-1777, 754) are navigation, not delete. The test (index.test.tsx:161-181) exercises only the keyboard {Delete} path. So a mouse- or touch-only user can select an event but has no affordance to delete it — the gap is real.

confirmedHigh.title: Standalone ChartTooltip exposes no formatter/auto-content API
confirmedHigh.detail: HeroUI Pro's ChartTooltip is the Recharts content renderer that itself accepts formatter, labelFormatter, indicator, hideLabel and reads active/payload. Vela's exported ChartTooltip is purely presentational and offloads all of that to LineChart.TooltipContent, so importing ChartTooltip directly gives none of the documented formatter-api/tooltip-content behavior.
confirmedHigh.evidence: src/components/chart-tooltip/index.tsx:6 `export type ChartTooltipProps = HTMLAttributes<HTMLDivElement>;` — no active/payload/formatter/labelFormatter props; the formatter API lives only in src/components/line-chart/index.tsx:190-202 (LineChartTooltipContentProps).
confirmedHigh.suggestedFix: Promote the active/payload/formatter/labelFormatter/indicator/hideLabel logic from LineChart.TooltipContent into a content-aware mode on ChartTooltip (e.g. ChartTooltip.Content) in src/components/chart-tooltip/index.tsx so the documented component itself satisfies the formatter/auto-content contract.
confirmedHigh.verifyReason: The code matches the claim. src/components/chart-tooltip/index.tsx:6 defines `export type ChartTooltipProps = HTMLAttributes<HTMLDivElement>;` and the component (lines 19-54) is purely presentational: a root div plus `Header`/`Item` slots, with no active/payload/formatter/labelFormatter/hideLabel API at the root (the only formatter-ish prop is per-Item `indicator`/`indicatorColor`, lines 10-17). The Recharts content-renderer behavior — reading active/payload, returning null when inactive, applying formatter/labelFormatter/hideLabel/indicator — lives in LineChart.TooltipContent (src/components/line-chart/index.tsx:190-234: LineChartTooltipContentProps + the TooltipContent fn that guards `if (!active || !payload...) return null` and calls `formatter(item.value, item.name)`), and is duplicated per chart in AreaChart/BarChart/ComposedChart .TooltipContent. The demo (src/showcase/demos/charts-demos.tsx) confirms the split: formatter API only via LineChart.TooltipContent (lines 812-822), standalone ChartTooltip only with static hand-authored children (lines 850-855, 863-865). So importing ChartTooltip directly yields none of the formatter/auto-content contract — factually accurate. Caveat lowering severity: this is a deliberate, consistently-applied delegation pattern (every chart family ships a working formatter-aware *.TooltipContent), not broken behavior; and the claim's load-bearing premise — that HeroUI Pro's public ChartTooltip itself carries formatter/labelFormatter rather than also delegating — cannot be verified from this repo (no Pro source present).

confirmedHigh.title: Group selection API is single-select only; cannot model multi-toggle variants
confirmedHigh.detail: ItemCardGroupProps exposes selectedKey/defaultSelectedKey/onSelectionChange typed as a single key|null, and updateSelection clears any other selection. HeroUI's group covers multi-state variants (linked-accounts, notification-preferences, permission-levels, developer-settings) where several items are independently toggled, which this API cannot represent.
confirmedHigh.evidence: index.tsx:28-31 `selectedKey?: ItemCardGroupSelectionKey | null` / `onSelectionChange?: (key: ItemCardGroupSelectionKey | null)`; updateSelection at index.tsx:74-82 computes a single nextKey and never holds a set.
confirmedHigh.suggestedFix: Add a selectionMode='single'|'multiple' prop with Set/array-based selectedKeys/onSelectionChange (mirroring react-aria selection), keeping the single-key form as a convenience, so multi-toggle groups can be driven by the group rather than worked around per-card.
confirmedHigh.verifyReason: Read src/components/item-card-group/index.tsx in full. The group selection API is structurally single-select. Lines 20/28-31 type the keys as a single scalar: `ItemCardGroupSelectionKey = string | number`, `selectedKey?: ItemCardGroupSelectionKey | null`, `defaultSelectedKey?: ... | null`, `onSelectionChange?: (key: ItemCardGroupSelectionKey | null) => void` — never a Set/array. Internal state is a single value: `useState<ItemCardGroupSelectionKey | null>(defaultSelectedKey ?? null)` (line 64-65) and `currentSelectedKey` (line 70). updateSelection (lines 74-82) computes one `nextKey` and per-card selection is derived as `currentSelectedKey === itemKey` (line 105), so at most one card can be selected via the group; selecting another replaces it. There is no `selectionMode` prop anywhere (props block lines 22-32). The test suite only exercises single selection ("drives single selection via onSelectionChange" line 70; "honors controlled selectedKey" line 92) with no multi-select coverage. The auditor's own concession that multi-toggle is achievable per-card holds (ItemCard exposes isSelected/defaultSelected/onSelectedChange at item-card/index.tsx:25-27), but that is exactly the "worked around per-card" path the claim describes — the GROUP API itself cannot represent multi-toggle. Severity high is appropriate: this is a controlled-API surface limitation, not a crash; multi-state variants can still be built by driving cards individually, just not coordinated by the group.

confirmedHigh.title: confidence-state has no implementation
confirmedHigh.detail: The contract lists a confidence-state token but neither ChatSourceProps nor ChatSourcePreviewProps expose any confidence/relevance prop, and no DOM/aria reflects a confidence level. The only state rendered is the transient `data-opened`/'已打开' open-feedback, which is a custom Vela addition, not a confidence indicator.
confirmedHigh.evidence: ChatSourceProps (index.tsx lines 27-32) and ChatSourcePreviewProps (lines 34-40) define only href/title/iconSrc/fallback/description plus OpenFeedbackProps; grep for 'confidence' in index.tsx returns nothing. data-opened (lines 98, 163) is the only state attribute.
confirmedHigh.suggestedFix: Add a `confidence`/`relevance` prop and surface it as a `data-confidence` attribute plus a visible indicator inside the source card and popover (e.g. a labeled dot/score) so the confidence-state contract is actually represented.
confirmedHigh.verifyReason: The gap is real. The contract (scripts/pro-parity-contract.cjs:42) lists `'chat-source': ['source-card', 'popover-details', 'confidence-state']`, but no confidence implementation exists. In src/components/chat-source/index.tsx: ChatSourceProps (lines 27-32) exposes only href/title/iconSrc/fallback + OpenFeedbackProps; ChatSourcePreviewProps (lines 34-40) exposes only href/title/description/iconSrc/onClick + OpenFeedbackProps; OpenFeedbackProps (lines 18-25) is onOpen/openedLabel/openFeedbackDuration. The only rendered state attribute is data-opened (lines 98, 163), driven by useOpenFeedback (lines 42-70) which toggles the transient '已打开' open-feedback (line 79) — a custom Vela affordance, not a confidence indicator. grep for confidence|relevance|score|data-confidence across src/components/chat-source/ returns nothing. The test (index.test.tsx) is only an axe smoke test, and the demo (ai-overlay-demos.tsx lines 104-131, 2068-2118) passes no confidence prop. So no prop, DOM attribute, aria, or visible indicator represents confidence-state anywhere. Minor caveat: confidence-state is a somewhat loosely-derived contract token for what is fundamentally a source-citation card, but it is genuinely unrepresented, so high severity stands.

confirmedHigh.title: month-overflow 'N more' jumps to day view instead of revealing hidden events
confirmedHigh.detail: Clicking the 'N more' chip calls setDate(date)+setView('day') — the same as clicking the date number — rather than opening an overflow popover that lists the hidden events for that day, which is the standard month-overflow behavior.
confirmedHigh.evidence: MonthCell handleMoreClick (L1739-1743) is identical to handleDateClick: pendingViewFocusRef.current=true; setDate(date); setView('day'). No popover/listbox of overflowed events is rendered.
confirmedHigh.suggestedFix: In MonthCell, render the overflow chip as a Popover trigger that lists the remaining (events.length - maxEvents) MonthEvent items, rather than navigating away to the day view.
confirmedHigh.verifyReason: Confirmed in src/components/agenda/index.tsx. The "N more" overflow chip (L1769-1778, data-slot="agenda-month-cell-more") wires onClick={handleMoreClick}. handleMoreClick (L1739-1743) is identical to handleDateClick (L1731-1737): both do `pendingViewFocusRef.current = true; setDate(date); setView('day')`. So clicking the chip just navigates to the day view, exactly like clicking the date number — the chip is redundant. grep for Popover/Listbox/popover in the agenda component returns zero hits; the only overflow rendering is `overflow = events.length - visibleEvents.length` (L1728) feeding the chip label (L1776). No popover/listbox of the hidden MonthEvent items is rendered in place, which is the standard month-overflow behavior (react-big-calendar/FullCalendar/Google Calendar). The demo (src/showcase/demos/data-display-demos.tsx L1229-1238) uses MonthCell with no Popover wrapper, so nothing supplies the behavior externally. Severity nuance: the hidden events are still reachable via the resulting day view, so this is a wrong-interaction-model/redundant-control parity divergence, not data loss — consistent with high/missing-interaction, not critical.

confirmedHigh.title: event-drag/event-resize absent in month view
confirmedHigh.detail: Month-view events (MonthEvent and MonthSpanningEvent) wire only onClick + handleKeyDown; they have no onPointerDown/startInteraction, so events cannot be dragged to a new day or resized in month view, unlike the week/day TimeGrid which fully supports it.
confirmedHigh.evidence: MonthEvent (L1882-1896) and MonthSpanningEvent (L1824-1843) render a <button> with onClick/onKeyDown only — no pointer drag handlers, no onEventMove wiring; startInteraction exists solely inside the timed Event component (L1423).
confirmedHigh.suggestedFix: Add pointer-based drag (and where applicable resize) to MonthSpanningEvent/MonthEvent in index.tsx that maps horizontal movement to day columns and calls onEventMove, mirroring the TimeGrid Event interaction.
confirmedHigh.verifyReason: The gap is real. MonthSpanningEvent (src/components/agenda/index.tsx L1794-1845) pulls only {selectedEventId, setSelectedEventId, onEventDelete} from context (L1801) and renders a <button> (L1825-1843) wiring only onClick (L1830) + onKeyDown (L1831). MonthEvent (L1854-1897) is identical: context destructure L1855, button L1883-1895 with only onClick (L1889) + onKeyDown (L1890). Neither destructures onEventMove/onEventResize nor attaches onPointerDown. Their keyDown handlers (L1807-1822, L1865-1880) support only Enter/Space (select) and Delete/Backspace (delete) — no move/resize. By contrast onEventMove/onEventResize ARE in context (AgendaContextValue L565-566) and are consumed only by the timed Event component (L1268-1270), which computes canMove/canResize (L1289-1290), defines startInteraction (L1423) that calls onEventMove/onEventResize on pointerup (L1417/L1419), and wires onPointerDown for move (L1551) and a resize handle (L1571). startInteraction is hard-bound to [data-slot=\"agenda-day-column\"] (L1429), which exists only in the TimeGrid, not the month grid. MonthCell adds no event drag (its onPointerDown at L1053/L1138 belongs to DayColumn's create-by-drag, calling onEventCreate, L1136-1196). index.test.tsx has zero month-view drag/resize coverage. So month-view events cannot be dragged to another day or resized, unlike week/day. Calibration: month-view resize is only meaningful for multi-day spanning events, not single-day MonthEvent — the auditor's \"where applicable\" already accounts for this; the drag-to-new-day move gap is unambiguously real.

confirmedHigh.title: Global Cmd+K / Ctrl+K open shortcut is decorative only — no keydown listener
confirmedHigh.detail: HeroUI Pro's command palette is opened by a global Cmd+K (Ctrl+K) shortcut, the defining behavior behind the keyboard-search contract token. Here open state is fully delegated to the consumer and the only Cmd+K reference is a visual <Kbd abbr="⌘">K</Kbd> on the trigger button — no component or demo wires a document keydown listener, so pressing Cmd+K does nothing.
confirmedHigh.evidence: Demo shows '<Kbd abbr="⌘" abbrTitle="Command">K</Kbd>' next to handleOpen, but grep for 'keydown|addEventListener|metaKey|useEffect' returns nothing in both index.tsx and the demo; open is only triggered by Button onClick={handleOpen}.
confirmedHigh.suggestedFix: Add an optional global hotkey to the Command root (e.g. an openShortcut prop / useEffect document keydown handler matching metaKey||ctrlKey + 'k' that calls onOpenChange(true) and preventDefault), so the advertised Cmd+K actually opens the palette like HeroUI Pro.

confirmedHigh.title: Nav items render no real <a> anchor — client routing loses native link behavior
confirmedHigh.detail: MenuItem renders a RAC TreeItem (role="row") and intercepts href imperatively in onAction via navigate()/window.location.href, so there is no rendered anchor. HeroUI Pro sidebar items are real links, so cmd/ctrl/middle-click 'open in new tab', right-click 'copy link', and hover status-bar URL preview all fail here.
confirmedHigh.evidence: `<TreeItem ... onAction={handleAction} data-href={href}>` with `navigate(href)` / `window.location.href = href` and no `<a href>` rendered (lines 407-432).
confirmedHigh.suggestedFix: Render an inner <a href={href}> (or RAC Link / RouterProvider integration) inside MenuItemContent so native modifier-click/new-tab/copy-link work, while keeping onAction for SPA navigation.

confirmedHigh.title: Mobile sidebar has no sheet/overlay — just opens a static div
confirmedHigh.detail: The aside gets a real drag-to-close Sheet on mobile, but the sidebar does not; MenuToggle merely flips open state and the sidebar stays the same static `app-layout__sidebar` div, so on mobile there is no scrim, no dismissable overlay, and no swipe-to-close as HeroUI Pro provides.
confirmedHigh.evidence: `MenuToggle` handlePress only does `ctx?.setSidebarOpen(true)`; `sidebarRegion` is a plain `<div className="app-layout__sidebar">` with no Sheet wrapper, while aside has `AppLayoutMobileAsideSheet` with `<Sheet placement="right">`.
confirmedHigh.suggestedFix: Render the sidebar inside a Sheet (mirroring AppLayoutMobileAsideSheet) when below the mobile breakpoint, driven by isSidebarOpen/setSidebarOpen, so mobile gets a real overlay with backdrop + drag/dismiss.

otherGaps.title: Test suite only asserts axe, never the tooltip-hover contract
otherGaps.detail: index.test.tsx contains a single test that renders the chart and runs axe; it never simulates hover, never asserts the ChartTooltip DOM appears, and never checks payload/value rendering. The core 'tooltip-hover' contract token is therefore completely unverified by the regression suite.
otherGaps.evidence: index.test.tsx lines 23-35: the only `it(...)` is 'has no axe a11y violations'; there is no fireEvent/userEvent, no query for role='tooltip' or '.chart-tooltip', no MouseEnter on the Radar series.
otherGaps.suggestedFix: Add a test that triggers the recharts tooltip (e.g., dispatch mouse move over the chart surface or render TooltipContent directly with active/payload props) and asserts the ChartTooltip header/item DOM and formatted value render.

otherGaps.title: No test coverage for the timeline component
otherGaps.detail: src/components/timeline/index.test.tsx does not exist, so none of the contract behaviors (axis/placement data-attrs, status mapping, isCurrent, pulse, Actions rendering, marker status fallback to item status) are regression-locked, unlike the rest of the library which is built on a vitest+Testing Library suite.
otherGaps.evidence: ls of src/components/timeline/ shows only index.tsx (6488 bytes); find over src for *.test.tsx referencing timeline returns nothing.
otherGaps.suggestedFix: Add index.test.tsx asserting data-axis/data-placement/data-size/data-density on the ol, data-status precedence between Item and Marker, data-current/data-pulse, resolvedAlign for start/end/alternate, and that Actions renders interactive children.

otherGaps.title: Chart-level pointer/click handlers cannot reach the recharts chart
otherGaps.detail: AreaChartRoot only forwards data/height/width/margin/children to <RechartsAreaChart>; all other props in ...rest are spread onto the outer wrapper <div>, never the chart. So onClick/onMouseMove with recharts' active-payload semantics (data-point selection, drill-down) are unreachable, unlike the sibling bar-chart which explicitly destructures and forwards onClick/onMouseMove/onTouch* to the chart.
otherGaps.evidence: index.tsx:105-116 spreads {...rest} on the <div> and passes only `data={data} margin={margin}` to <RechartsAreaChart>; bar-chart/index.tsx:191-232 builds a chartProps object with onClick/onMouseEnter/onTouchStart etc. and spreads it onto <RechartsBarChart>.
otherGaps.suggestedFix: Mirror bar-chart: Pick the recharts chart-level handlers (onClick, onMouse*, onTouch*) plus accessibilityLayer/syncId/stackOffset/reverseStackOrder into a typed chartProps and spread them onto <RechartsAreaChart> instead of the wrapper div.

otherGaps.title: Test coverage is axe-only; no tooltip/hover/multi-series behavioral assertions
otherGaps.detail: index.test.tsx contains a single test (`has no axe a11y violations`) and never asserts that the tooltip content renders on hover, that multiple series draw, that the formatter/labelFormatter/hideLabel props of TooltipContent work, or that responsive sizing/sparkline variant behave. The four contract behaviors (responsive-chart, tooltip-hover, multi-series, sparkline) are implemented but untested.
otherGaps.evidence: index.test.tsx has exactly 1 `it(` block; grep for hover/mouse/fireEvent/userEvent finds only the static `<LineChart.Tooltip content=...>` JSX on line 30, no interaction is fired.
otherGaps.suggestedFix: Add tests that render TooltipContent directly with active/payload props (jsdom can't drive SVG hover) to assert header label, per-series items with indicatorColor, and formatter/labelFormatter/hideLabel output; assert multiple Recharts line paths render for multi-series data.

otherGaps.title: Highlighting is a regex tokenizer, not Shiki, and silently no-ops for unsupported languages
otherGaps.detail: HeroUI Pro uses Shiki for language-aware AST highlighting; Vela ships a hand-written regex tokenizer (TYPE_SCRIPT_RULES/JSON_RULES/SHELL_RULES) that only covers json/shell/javascript/typescript. getHighlightRules returns [] for any other language, so the documented 'language' prop produces unstyled plain text for e.g. python/css/html/tsx-with-jsx etc.
otherGaps.evidence: index.tsx:110-118 getHighlightRules returns JSON/SHELL/TYPE_SCRIPT rules only and falls through to `return []`; comment line 23 admits '本仓不内置 Shiki，按基准快照渲染纯 pre>code'.
otherGaps.suggestedFix: Either integrate Shiki (matching HeroUI) for full language coverage, or document the supported-language subset and ensure the regex tokenizer covers the languages the demos/registry actually use; at minimum render code unbroken (it does) but the parity is partial for arbitrary languages.

otherGaps.title: Footer/preview area is dead code — unreachable from the public API
otherGaps.detail: HeroUI Pro exposes a footer/preview content area below the grid (used for the selected-emoji preview and skin-tone controls). Panel supports a `footer` prop and renders an emoji-picker-footer slot, but EmojiPicker never passes it and it is not part of the public props, so no consumer can ever populate it.
otherGaps.evidence: `footer?: ReactNode` on PanelProps (line 117) and the render block (lines 229-233) exist, but both Panel call sites omit footer (inline lines 285-291, popover lines 316-322) and `footer` is absent from EmojiPickerProps (lines 36-54). grep for `footer={` returns nothing.
otherGaps.suggestedFix: Either surface a `footer`/`preview` prop on EmojiPickerProps and forward it to Panel, or render a built-in selected-emoji preview footer; otherwise remove the dead Panel.footer prop.

otherGaps.title: No Legend subcomponent for multi-series / stacked area charts
otherGaps.detail: The multi-series and stacked contract relies on distinguishing series, but AreaChart exposes no Legend. The multi-area and stacked demos render two/three Area series with no legend affordance, so series are only identifiable via hover tooltip. Sibling bar-chart exposes Legend (and Brush/ReferenceLine/Cell/Label/LabelList).
otherGaps.evidence: index.tsx:122-129 Object.assign(AreaChartRoot, { Area, XAxis, YAxis, Grid, Tooltip, TooltipContent }) has no Legend; bar-chart/index.tsx:242-257 includes Legend, Brush, ReferenceLine, Cell, Label, LabelList. The stacked demo (charts-demos.tsx:561-593) renders 3 Area series with no legend.
otherGaps.suggestedFix: Add recharts `Legend` (and likely ReferenceLine/Label) to the AreaChart Object.assign in index.tsx:122-129 so multi-series/stacked demos can render a legend like the HeroUI reference and the bar-chart sibling.

otherGaps.title: No chart-level event handlers or sync/stack props are forwarded to the engine
otherGaps.detail: ComposedChartProps is `Omit<HTMLAttributes<HTMLDivElement>,'className'|'style'>` plus data/height/width/margin/children, and `...rest` is spread onto the wrapper <div> (index.tsx:61), not the chart. So consumers cannot wire onClick/onMouseMove/onTouchStart on chart data, nor pass syncId/syncMethod, stackOffset, reverseStackOrder, throttleDelay or cursor to the chart. bar-chart/line-chart expose all of these, leaving composed-chart's API materially thinner than its siblings and than HeroUI Pro's interactive composed charts.
otherGaps.evidence: index.tsx:27-40 props shape lacks any chart-level handler/sync props; index.tsx:57-62 spreads `{...rest}` on the <div>; bar-chart/index.tsx:203-220 forwards onClick/onMouseMove/onTouchStart/syncId/syncMethod/stackOffset/etc. into the Recharts chart.
otherGaps.suggestedFix: Mirror the bar-chart pattern: add the Recharts chart-level props (event handlers, syncId/syncMethod, stackOffset, reverseStackOrder, cursor, throttleDelay) to ComposedChartProps and pass them to <RechartsComposedChart> instead of letting only DOM attrs reach the div.

otherGaps.title: Slice hover does not drive any highlight; only legend hover does
otherGaps.detail: In the with-breakdown demo, the visual highlight (dimming other slices, thickening stroke, KPI/tooltip panel) is computed from hoveredChannel, but hoveredChannel is updated solely by InteractiveLegendItem's onHover (charts-demos.tsx:441-442). The PieChart.Pie itself wires no onMouseEnter/onMouseLeave, so hovering a chart slice produces no active state.
otherGaps.evidence: onMouseEnter/onMouseLeave appear only on InteractiveLegendItem (charts-demos.tsx:441-442) and as unused chart-level passthroughs (index.tsx:154-155,190-191); no PieChart.Pie in any demo sets onMouseEnter.
otherGaps.suggestedFix: Wire PieChart.Pie onMouseEnter/onMouseLeave (or activeIndex via the chart-level onMouseMove) to the highlight state so pointer interaction on the slices, not just the legend, triggers the active-slice visual.

otherGaps.title: Copy icon swap is instant; the Motion enter/exit animation is replaced by a hardcoded static style
otherGaps.detail: HeroUI animates the copy/check icon transition (the wrapper carries Motion filter/opacity/transform). Vela renders a span with ICON_MOTION_STYLE permanently fixed to the terminal frame {filter:'blur(0px)',opacity:1,transform:'none'}, so getCopyIcon swaps the SVG with no fade/blur/scale transition.
otherGaps.evidence: index.tsx:222 `const ICON_MOTION_STYLE = { filter:'blur(0px)', opacity:1, transform:'none' }` applied at line 363; comment line 221 admits '本仓无动画库，静态对齐'. getCopyIcon (258-262) just returns a different SVG with no transition wrapper.
otherGaps.suggestedFix: Drive the icon swap through a CSS keyframe/transition (opacity+blur) keyed on data-copy-status, or use the project's real animation library, instead of pinning a static terminal-frame inline style.

otherGaps.title: Documented Panel prop groupResizeBehavior is not part of the typed API
otherGaps.detail: HeroUI Pro documents Resizable.Panel's groupResizeBehavior ('preserve-relative-size' | 'preserve-pixel-size'), which the underlying engine supports as a first-class PanelProp. Vela's Panel never destructures or types it, so a consumer cannot pass it in a type-safe way (it would only sneak through ...rest untyped).
otherGaps.evidence: src/components/resizable/index.tsx ResizablePanelProps (lines 59-86) lists defaultSize/minSize/maxSize/collapsible/collapsedSize/disabled/onResize/etc. but no groupResizeBehavior; the type is Omit<HTMLAttributes<...>> & {...}, and groupResizeBehavior is not an HTMLAttribute, so TS rejects it. Engine d.ts line 294 declares groupResizeBehavior?: 'preserve-relative-size' | 'preserve-pixel-size'.
otherGaps.suggestedFix: Add `groupResizeBehavior?: 'preserve-relative-size' | 'preserve-pixel-size'` to ResizablePanelProps, destructure it in Panel, and forward it explicitly to <RPanel> in src/components/resizable/index.tsx (line ~349).

otherGaps.title: ListView.EmptyState is a disconnected styled div, not wired to the grid's renderEmptyState
otherGaps.detail: HeroUI Pro documents empty state via the renderEmptyState prop (custom render when the list has no items). Vela exports ListView.EmptyState (index.tsx:158-168) as a plain forwardRef <div data-slot="list-view-empty-state">, but it is never connected to GridList — GridList only shows empty content through its renderEmptyState callback (RAC GridListProps line 78), which this subcomponent does not feed. A consumer placing <ListView.EmptyState> as a child of <ListView> would have it discarded by RAC's collection (only Items are collected), so the library's named empty-state primitive is non-functional.
otherGaps.evidence: index.tsx:158-168 defines ListViewEmptyState as a loose div with no relation to GridList; no demo passes renderEmptyState or uses ListView.EmptyState (grep of data-display-demos.tsx shows ListView.EmptyState never instantiated).
otherGaps.suggestedFix: Make ListView.EmptyState render through the grid: either auto-pass a renderEmptyState that wraps children, or document/wire it so it appears inside GridList's empty render slot (e.g. accept a renderEmptyState passthrough or have the Root inject one). Add a demo that shows the empty list state.

otherGaps.title: Scrollable code region is not keyboard-focusable/scrollable
otherGaps.detail: The contract token scrolling-code implies a scrollable code area. The code div has overflow-x:auto (runtime CSS line 10950) but no tabIndex and no role/aria-label, so keyboard-only users cannot focus or scroll the overflow region, and screen readers get no region semantics.
otherGaps.evidence: index.tsx:280-292 the Code div renders only data-slot/className; no tabIndex={0}, role, or aria-label. styles.css:10945-10951 `.code-block__code { overflow-x:auto }`.
otherGaps.suggestedFix: Add tabIndex={0} (when content overflows) plus role="region" with an aria-label (e.g. derived from language) to the code-block__code div so the scroll area is keyboard-operable and announced.

otherGaps.title: Size props typed as number-only, dropping documented CSS-unit string sizes
otherGaps.detail: HeroUI Pro documents Panel sizes as 'numbers as percentages, strings as CSS units', and the engine accepts number | string for defaultSize/minSize/maxSize/collapsedSize (e.g. '200px'). Vela narrows all of these to number, so pixel/CSS-unit sizing (the documented 'size units' capability) cannot be expressed through the typed API.
otherGaps.evidence: src/components/resizable/index.tsx lines 65-74 declare defaultSize?: number; minSize?: number; maxSize?: number; collapsedSize?: number. Engine d.ts lines 261/274/305/309 all declare these as number | string | undefined.
otherGaps.suggestedFix: Widen defaultSize/minSize/maxSize/collapsedSize to `number | string` in ResizablePanelProps so consumers can pass CSS-unit strings, which RPanel already forwards.

otherGaps.title: Hide-on-scroll and menu open/close transitions ignore prefers-reduced-motion
otherGaps.detail: The docs/contract state animations respect prefers-reduced-motion, but the two primary animations are applied via inline JS styles: the hideOnScroll transform/transition (index.tsx:178-180) and the menu height/opacity transition (index.tsx:499). The only reduced-motion rule is in navbar.css:319-324 and targets only the hamburger icon pseudo-elements; a CSS media query cannot override an inline transition without !important, so motion-sensitive users still get the full slide/expand animations.
otherGaps.evidence: index.tsx:179 'transition: transform var(--navbar-transition-duration,0.3s) ease'; index.tsx:499 'transition: height 300ms ease, opacity 300ms ease'; navbar.css reduced-motion block (line 319) only zeroes .navbar__menu-toggle-icon transitions.
otherGaps.suggestedFix: Read prefers-reduced-motion via matchMedia in NavbarRoot/Menu and set the inline transition to 'none' (and skip the two-phase reflow animation) when reduced motion is requested.

otherGaps.title: Progress-ring variant renders a full arc instead of 72% because it omits the angle-axis domain
otherGaps.detail: recharts scales a RadialBar relative to the max datum value; for the single-datum RADIAL_PROGRESS_DATA ([{value:72}]) the bar fills the entire arc unless a PolarAngleAxis with domain={[0,100]} is present. The progress-ring demo (charts-demos.tsx:1518-1523) has no AngleAxis, so '72%' draws as a complete ring, breaking the progress-arc contract; the gauge variant (1499-1505) correctly sets type="number" domain={[0,100]}.
otherGaps.evidence: charts-demos.tsx:1518-1523 RadialChart with RADIAL_PROGRESS_DATA value:72 and NO RadialChart.AngleAxis, vs charts-demos.tsx:1499-1505 which includes domain={[0,100]}.
otherGaps.suggestedFix: Add a RadialChart.AngleAxis type="number" domain={[0,100]} tick={false} to the progress-ring demo so the single-value bar fills proportionally to its value rather than the full circle.

otherGaps.title: column.width silently ignored unless enableColumnResizing is set
otherGaps.detail: The Column `width` prop is forwarded straight to RAC's Column, which per RAC's own type docs only applies when wrapped in a ResizableTableContainer. In the non-resizable Table.ScrollContainer path (enableColumnResizing=false), every column.width is silently dropped, contradicting the documented API ("初始/受控列宽 px/%/fr").
otherGaps.evidence: index.tsx line 1449 passes `width={getColumnWidth(column) ?? ...}` on Table.Column unconditionally, but line 1505-1511 only wraps in Table.ResizableContainer when enableColumnResizing is true (else Table.ScrollContainer). RAC Table.d.ts line 214-217: width "only applies when the <Table> is wrapped in a ResizableTableContainer". Demos like the drag-and-drop columns (line 2131 width:56) and SERVER_COLUMNS (line 2369 width:100) set width without enableColumnResizing.
otherGaps.suggestedFix: When enableColumnResizing is false, emit the configured width as inline style/colgroup on the th/td (e.g. style width + min/max) instead of relying on RAC's width prop, so declared column widths take effect outside the resizable container.

otherGaps.title: LinkPopover ignores the existing link on the selection
otherGaps.detail: HeroUI Pro's link popover pre-fills the URL of an already-linked selection and lets you edit it. Here the input always defaults to a hardcoded vela-ui.local URL and never inspects the selection's anchor href, so editing an existing link starts from the wrong value.
otherGaps.evidence: src/components/rich-text-editor/index.tsx:540 `useState('https://vela-ui.local/docs/rich-text-editor')` and the popover never reads document.queryCommandValue('createLink') or the selected <a> href.
otherGaps.suggestedFix: On popover open, read the anchor href under the current selection (via lastSelectionRef / closest('a')) and seed the input with it, defaulting to empty otherwise.

otherGaps.title: Root never forwards accessibilityLayer, so chart tooltips are pointer-only (no keyboard access)
otherGaps.detail: ComposedChartRoot (index.tsx:44-70) only passes data, margin and children to <RechartsComposedChart>; it never sets or forwards accessibilityLayer, so Recharts' built-in keyboard navigation of data points / tooltips is off. Sibling bar-chart and line-chart explicitly forward accessibilityLayer to their charts, so composed-chart diverges from the library's own accessible-chart convention and from HeroUI Pro's tooltip-hover-with-keyboard contract.
otherGaps.evidence: index.tsx:64 `<RechartsComposedChart data={data} margin={margin}>` (no accessibilityLayer); contrast bar-chart/index.tsx:151,192,232 which threads `accessibilityLayer` into chartProps spread onto <RechartsBarChart {...chartProps}>.
otherGaps.suggestedFix: Add an `accessibilityLayer` prop to ComposedChartProps (defaulting true, matching bar/line) and forward it to <RechartsComposedChart>, so tooltips are reachable via keyboard arrow navigation.

otherGaps.title: No keyboard/a11y access to slices; accessibilityLayer never enabled
otherGaps.detail: accessibilityLayer is a passthrough prop (index.tsx:138,176) but no demo sets it, and the SVG slices carry no per-slice role/aria or keyboard focus. There is no way to reach a slice or its tooltip without a pointer.
otherGaps.evidence: accessibilityLayer is destructured/forwarded (index.tsx:138,176) but absent from all PieChart usages in charts-demos.tsx; the only test is an axe smoke test with no role/keyboard assertions (index.test.tsx:24-32).
otherGaps.suggestedFix: Enable accessibilityLayer in demos (and consider keyboard-driven activeIndex) so slices/tooltip are reachable via keyboard, matching recharts' accessible chart pattern.

otherGaps.title: Inline-editable columns are always an open <input>; custom cell renderer and read-only display state are bypassed
otherGaps.detail: For an editable column without a custom `editor`, the cell is permanently rendered as a bare <input defaultValue={inlineInputValue}> rather than showing formatted/read-only content with a click-to-edit affordance. This ignores column.cell entirely for such columns and means there is no non-editing display state, diverging from a typical data-grid cell-editing UX where the cell shows rendered content until activated.
otherGaps.evidence: index.tsx lines 1285-1301: branch `isEditable && column.editor === undefined ? inlineEditorNode : ...`; inlineEditorNode (lines 1268-1284) is an always-mounted <input defaultValue={inlineInputValue}> built from formatCellValue, never calling renderCell(item, column) and never honoring column.cell.
otherGaps.suggestedFix: Render renderCell(item, column) inside the editable-cell trigger by default and only swap to the <input> editor on activation (as already done for the custom-editor button path), instead of permanently mounting an input for inline-editable columns.

otherGaps.title:
Description renders as
, not a
otherGaps.detail:
Reference renders EmptyState.Description as a

; the Vela Description (index.tsx:57-59) renders a generic

. This diverges from the documented DOM and drops paragraph semantics for the supporting copy.
otherGaps.evidence: index.tsx:57-59 `<div ref={ref} className={clsx('empty-state__description', className)} {...rest} />`; reference doc states "Description is rendered as a p tag".
otherGaps.suggestedFix:
Render Description as a

element (HTMLParagraphElement) while keeping the empty-state__description class and forwarded props.

otherGaps.title: Trigger lacks aria-controls / aria-haspopup linking it to the popover panel
otherGaps.detail: The trigger toggles a popover (aria-expanded is set, line 226) but never advertises that it controls a popup nor which element. Without aria-haspopup and aria-controls pointing at the Content panel id, the relationship between trigger and the TOC menu is not exposed to assistive tech.
otherGaps.evidence: Trigger button (index.tsx:222-240) sets aria-expanded={isOpen} but has no aria-haspopup or aria-controls; Content (index.tsx:351) sets no id and the trigger references none.
otherGaps.suggestedFix: Add aria-haspopup="true" and aria-controls={contentId} on the trigger, and set a matching id on the Popover/Content, generated/shared via context.

otherGaps.title: DropIndicator is exported but never rendered during a drag (no live insertion feedback)
otherGaps.detail: Kanban.DropIndicator exists as a component and is part of the public surface, but CardList never renders it during dragging and no demo uses it. There is no visual insertion line showing where a dragged card will land, which HeroUI Pro shows during drag-over.
otherGaps.evidence: src/components/kanban/index.tsx:507-521 defines KanbanDropIndicator; grep shows it is referenced only in index.tsx (definition + Object.assign at 805) and never consumed by CardList or any demo in src/showcase/demos/.
otherGaps.suggestedFix: Track the active drag-over insertion index in CardList and render Kanban.DropIndicator at that slot (or rely on Motion layout placeholder), so users get the documented drop-position affordance.

otherGaps.title: Action silently discards isIconOnly/size/variant despite 'Extends HeroUI Button props'
otherGaps.detail: ChatMessageActions.Action's doc says it extends HeroUI Button props, but it destructures and throws away isIconOnly, size, and variant, hardcoding icon-only/sm/ghost. A consumer passing variant="solid" or size="md" (valid per the contract) gets no effect.
otherGaps.evidence: index.tsx:200-213 `({ className, isIconOnly: _isIconOnly, size: _size, variant: _variant, ...rest }, ref) => (<Button ... isIconOnly size="sm" variant="ghost" ...>` — the override props are renamed to underscore vars and never forwarded.
otherGaps.suggestedFix: Forward consumer-provided isIconOnly/size/variant with the icon-only/sm/ghost values as defaults (e.g. `variant = 'ghost'` default param) instead of discarding them, so Action truly extends Button props.

otherGaps.title: No image (![]()) rendering
otherGaps.detail: Standard markdown/GFM images are unsupported: img is absent from the element union and parseInline has no image branch, so ![alt](src) renders as literal text — react-markdown (the documented engine) renders images.
otherGaps.evidence: MarkdownElementName union (index.tsx:15-41) has no 'img'; parseInline regex (index.tsx:108) handles only code/strong/del/link/em and grep for img/image/![ in the file returned no matches.
otherGaps.suggestedFix: Add an image token to the parseInline regex and render an (with alt/src) via renderElement in src/components/markdown/index.tsx.

otherGaps.title: Indicator SVG has no intrinsic size at runtime
otherGaps.detail: The arrow SVGs declare only viewBox (no width/height attributes), and the only icon-sizing CSS (trend-chip--sm/md/lg .trend-chip__indicator) lives in the non-loaded reference partial. With heroui-full.css having no trend-chip rules, the indicator icon has no defined dimensions at runtime and will collapse or fall back to the SVG default size.
otherGaps.evidence: index.tsx:22-38 SVGs use `viewBox="0 0 24 24"` with no width/height; trend-chip.css:19-32 holds the size-specific indicator dimensions, but heroui-full.css contains no trend-chip selectors.
