otherGaps.suggestedFix: Give the SVGs explicit width/height (per size) or apply sizing via inline style/em units in index.tsx so the indicator renders correctly without depending on the non-loaded CSS partial.

otherGaps.title: CellSlider.Label is a detached <span>, not associated with the slider's accessible name
otherGaps.detail: CellSlider.Label renders a bare <span data-slot="cell-slider-label"> instead of HeroUI/RAC's Label primitive; the inline comment at index.tsx:90 explicitly states it '不走 OSS Slider 的 label 槽' (does not use the OSS Slider label slot). RAC's Slider provides a LabelContext/labelProps that auto-wires a child <Label> to the slider group via aria-labelledby (react-aria-components Slider falls back to a generated name only when neither aria-label nor aria-labelledby is present). Because the visible label is detached, the slider is only accessible when the consumer also passes aria-label — which every demo/test does (forms-demos.tsx:889 aria-label='字间距'; index.test.tsx:9 aria-label='volume'). A consumer who relies on the visible <CellSlider.Label> alone (the natural expectation for a labeled settings cell) gets an unnamed slider.
otherGaps.evidence: index.tsx:91-98 renders Label as a plain <span> with clsx('cell-slider__label'); no useId/id/aria-labelledby anywhere in index.tsx; comment line 90: 'Label 是普通 span...不走 OSS Slider 的 label 槽'. RAC Slider exposes LabelContext (private/Slider.mjs:2,49,87).
otherGaps.suggestedFix: In CellSlider.Label, generate an id (useId) and set it on the span, then thread that id into the root Slider as aria-labelledby (e.g. via context) so the visible label names the slider; or render through HeroUI's Label primitive within the Slider so RAC auto-associates it. Keep aria-label as an override.

otherGaps.title: Escape key does not close the open mobile menu
otherGaps.detail: When the mobile Menu is open it behaves like an expandable overlay panel (absolute, covers viewport height via calc(100dvh - var(--navbar-height))), but there is no keydown handler anywhere in the component (grep for Escape/keyDown returns nothing). Pressing Escape does not call setMenuOpen(false), so keyboard users cannot dismiss the menu the standard way; closing only happens via the toggle or clicking a MenuItem.
otherGaps.evidence: No onKeyDown/Escape handling in index.tsx (grep returns empty); menu close paths are only setMenuOpen(false) inside MenuItem press (index.tsx:310) and the ToggleButton onChange (index.tsx:436).
otherGaps.suggestedFix: Add a document/keydown listener (or onKeyDown on the Menu) while isMenuOpen that calls setMenuOpen(false) on Escape, optionally returning focus to the MenuToggle.

otherGaps.title: Trend direction not exposed to assistive tech
otherGaps.detail: Direction is conveyed only by color (TREND_COLOR) and a decorative arrow marked aria-hidden, with no visually-hidden text or aria-label. A screen reader hears only the value (e.g. "12.5%") with no up/down/neutral context, breaking the trend-direction contract for non-sighted users.
otherGaps.evidence: index.tsx:23/29/35 each icon SVG has `aria-hidden="true"`; the chip (index.tsx:48-59) has no aria-label/role and no sr-only direction text; color mapping at lines 16-20 is the only direction signal.
otherGaps.suggestedFix: Add a visually-hidden text node or aria-label conveying the trend (e.g. "trending up") so the direction is announced independent of color/icon.

otherGaps.title: Root hardcodes role="status" (a live region) not in the reference
otherGaps.detail: The root forces role="status" (index.tsx:26), turning the empty state into an aria-live=polite region. The reference documents no ARIA role on the root div. For a persistent, always-rendered empty state this can cause spurious announcements on mount/re-render, and it cannot be overridden because the literal role is spread after {...rest} would only override if passed — but role is set before rest so a consumer-supplied role wins; still, the default semantics differ from HeroUI.
otherGaps.evidence: index.tsx:24-29 `<div ref={ref} role="status" className={clsx('empty-state', ...)} {...rest} />`; reference: "No explicit ARIA roles are documented for the root component."
otherGaps.suggestedFix: Drop the hardcoded role="status" (let the root be a plain div, or make role opt-in via prop) so behavior matches HeroUI and avoids unintended live-region announcements.

otherGaps.title: half-step is read-only display only; cannot be selected interactively
otherGaps.detail: The half-step contract implies users can pick half values. Here fractional values only render a read-only partial overlay; selection always maps to integer item values. handleChange does `Number(next)` where `next` is the item's integer value string (line 107 `value={String(value)}`), and selected is `Math.floor(rating)` (line 128), so onValueChange can never emit a .5 value via interaction.
otherGaps.evidence: src/components/rating/index.tsx line 128 `const selected = Math.floor(rating)`, line 107 `value={String(value)}` (integer item value), line 130-134 handleChange emits `Number(next)`; partial overlay (lines 88-96) renders only for the `isPartial` display case. No demo selects a half value — read-only-fractional variant uses a static `value={3.5}` with isReadOnly (feedback-nav-demos.tsx line 1345).
otherGaps.suggestedFix: Add an opt-in half-step selection mode (e.g. allowHalf prop) that splits each star into two pointer/keyboard targets so users can commit .5 values, with arrow keys stepping by 0.5; keep current integer behavior as default.

otherGaps.title: Editor textbox is not labeled and toolbar buttons lack tooltips/title
otherGaps.detail: The contenteditable role=textbox has aria-multiline but no aria-label/aria-labelledby, and toggle buttons render single-glyph text labels (B, I, U) with no title/aria-label by default, so screen readers announce only the glyph unless the demo passes aria-label.
otherGaps.evidence: src/components/rich-text-editor/index.tsx:629-634 Content has role=textbox/aria-multiline but no aria-label; ToggleButton (465-488) renders commandLabel glyph and supplies no default aria-label/title.
otherGaps.suggestedFix: Add an aria-label (or labelledby) to the Content textbox and provide default accessible names/titles on Toggle/Action buttons derived from the command so accessibility doesn't depend on each demo.

otherGaps.title: Grouped and stacked-favicons variants are not real behaviors
otherGaps.detail: HeroUI's Grouped variant collapses multiple citations behind a disclosure trigger, and Stacked Favicons shows layered/overlapping favicon avatars. The component provides no grouping/stacking primitive; the demos just map plain ChatSource items into a flex row (grouped) or inline into a message (stacked-favicons), with no disclosure and no avatar stacking.
otherGaps.evidence: ai-overlay-demos.tsx lines 2087-2102: variant==='grouped' renders SOURCE_ITEMS.map(...) inside `<div className="flex flex-wrap gap-2">`; variant==='stacked-favicons' renders the same map inline in a ChatMessage. index.tsx exports only ChatSourceRoot + Preview (line 195) — no Group/Stack subcomponent.
otherGaps.suggestedFix: Add a ChatSource.Group (collapses N sources behind a disclosure/+N trigger with popover list) and a stacked-favicon layout mode so these two HeroUI variants are real composed components rather than ad-hoc demo layouts.

otherGaps.title: In snap mode, dragging to EXPAND is rubber-band damped, making upward growth nearly impossible
otherGaps.detail: dragElastic damps the non-dismiss direction to 0.12 with dragConstraints pinned to 0. For a bottom snap sheet this means dragging UP to a larger snap point barely moves the panel (12% with rubber-band), which contradicts HeroUI snap sheets where dragging toward a larger snap point freely expands the panel.
otherGaps.evidence: index.tsx:402-409 sets `top: vertical ? (sign>0 ? 0.12 : 1)` (bottom placement → top elastic 0.12) and `dragConstraints={{top:0,bottom:0,left:0,right:0}}` (index.tsx:409); the elastic config is dismiss-oriented and not relaxed when snapsEnabled.
otherGaps.suggestedFix: When snapsEnabled, widen dragConstraints/elastic in the expand direction (e.g. allow free travel up to the largest snap point) so the panel visibly grows while dragging toward a bigger snap.

otherGaps.title: recharts Legend re-exported but never rendered in any pie variant
otherGaps.detail: PieChart.Legend is exposed (index.tsx:229) but no PieChart demo renders it; the only legend shown is Widget.Legend, a separate component. The documented 'legend' behavior of the pie chart itself is therefore unexercised and unverified.
otherGaps.evidence: grep 'PieChart.Legend' in charts-demos.tsx returns no matches; the legend lines (1167,1403) are <Widget.Legend>, not PieChart.Legend.
otherGaps.suggestedFix: Add a variant that renders PieChart.Legend (or document Widget.Legend as the intended composition) and verify it reflects/toggles slices, so the legend contract is demonstrated on the pie component itself.

otherGaps.title: No keyboard activation of the hover tooltip (accessibilityLayer never enabled, no role/aria on root)
otherGaps.detail: tooltip-hover is implemented only via recharts pointer events. accessibilityLayer is plumbed through (index.tsx:159,201) but defaults to undefined (recharts default false) and no demo enables it, so there is no arrow-key tooltip navigation; the root div (index.tsx:237-243) also has no role="img"/aria-label, leaving the chart opaque to screen readers.
otherGaps.evidence: index.tsx:201 'accessibilityLayer,' passed straight into chartProps with no default; root div className='radial-chart' has only data-slot, no role or aria-label; no demo passes accessibilityLayer (grep found none).
otherGaps.suggestedFix: Default accessibilityLayer to true on the RadialBarChart so keyboard focus/arrow keys surface the tooltip, and add a role="img" plus an aria-label (or aria-label prop) on the container div.

otherGaps.title: Slides lack per-slide accessible labels and no live region announces the active slide
otherGaps.detail: Each Carousel.Item gets role=group + aria-roledescription=slide (index.tsx:191-193) but no aria-label like "N of M", and there is no aria-live region anywhere (grep for aria-live returns nothing). HeroUI/APG carousels label each slide and announce slide changes for screen-reader users, especially since the track itself is not a listbox.
otherGaps.evidence: index.tsx:189-198 Item renders only role/aria-roledescription/data-slot/className; grep aria-live across index.tsx yields no match.
otherGaps.suggestedFix: Give each Item an aria-label of `${index+1} of ${count}` (count via context), and add an aria-live="polite" region (e.g. on the content track, switching to "off" during autoplay) so the current slide is announced on change.

otherGaps.title: TOC panel has no navigation/list semantics — it is a bare div of buttons
otherGaps.detail: HeroUI Pro's floating TOC is a navigation list of anchor links. Here Content is just the RAC Popover wrapping arbitrary children (line 351-368) and the demos render items inside a plain <div> (data-display-demos.tsx:2834-2844), so there is no role="navigation"/nav landmark and no list (ul/li or role=list) grouping. AT users get a flat run of buttons with no 'table of contents' structure.
otherGaps.evidence: Content returns <Popover ...>{children}</Popover> with no inner nav/ul; demo wraps items in <div style={...}> (data-display-demos.tsx:2834). No role= attribute exists anywhere in index.tsx.
otherGaps.suggestedFix: Have Content render a nav/list wrapper (e.g. role="navigation" aria-label="Table of contents" with a role="list"/role="listitem" structure, or a List component) around children so the panel is announced as a navigable TOC.

otherGaps.title: Card list uses role=group and cards have no list/grid item role
otherGaps.detail: The card list container is given role="group" while individual cards render as plain divs with only data-slot/data-key and no role, so assistive tech does not perceive a list of items the way HeroUI Pro's GridList-based kanban does (it exposes list/grid semantics). The component's own test comment flags the resulting axe aria-required-children concern.
otherGaps.evidence: src/components/kanban/index.tsx:300 (`role: 'group' as const`); Card render path (368-380 static, 386-405 draggable) sets no role on the card element; index.test.tsx:98-104 notes role=list without listitem children would violate axe.
otherGaps.suggestedFix: Give the list an appropriate list/grid role with cards as listitem/row+gridcell (or keep role=group consistently without implying list semantics), and add aria-roledescription for the draggable cards to match HeroUI Pro's grid semantics.

otherGaps.title: List variant never injects the documented auto-dividers between cards
otherGaps.detail: HeroUI's List item-card-group documents 'auto-dividers between cards'. The Root renders enhancedChildren directly with no separator between adjacent ItemCards, and ItemCard emits no divider; the only divider styling lives in src/styles/components/item-card-group.css ([data-slot=separator]) which targets a slot the component never produces and is not loaded at runtime.
otherGaps.evidence: index.tsx:133 `{enhancedChildren}` with no interleaved separator; grep for Separator/Divider in index.tsx returns nothing; item-card-group.css:53-60 styles [data-slot=separator] that is never rendered.
otherGaps.suggestedFix: In ItemCardGroupRoot, when layout='list', interleave a divider element (e.g. a <div data-slot="separator" role="separator"> or border-driven slot) between consecutive ItemCard children so the list shows the documented auto-dividers.

otherGaps.title: Kpi.Chart exposes no data/dataKey API — bare passthrough div
otherGaps.detail: HeroUI Pro's KPI.Chart renders an area/sparkline chart accepting data, dataKey, color, height and strokeWidth. Vela's Chart (index.tsx:60-63) is a plain div with only a CSS fade mask; every demo must hand-compose an AreaChart/Sparkline inside it. Composition works, but the documented chart API is absent.
otherGaps.evidence: index.tsx line 60: Chart renders <div className={clsx('kpi__chart', className)} {...rest}/> with no data/dataKey. Demos manually nest charts: <Kpi.Chart><AreaChart data={MONTHLY} .../></Kpi.Chart> (charts-demos.tsx:465-488) and <Kpi.Chart><SparklineLine values={...}/></Kpi.Chart> (data-display-demos.tsx:3461-3463).
otherGaps.suggestedFix: Either accept the composition pattern as the intended API and document it, or add optional data/dataKey/color/height props to Chart in index.tsx that render a default sparkline, matching the reference's prop surface.

otherGaps.title: Streaming state gives no per-step active indication and no leading status icon
otherGaps.detail: When isStreaming is true only the trigger text gets the shimmer; there is no leading status icon (spinner/sparkle) on the trigger and no way to mark which step is currently active, so streaming-state + status-icons are only partially conveyed versus HeroUI's combined streaming/agent-trace examples.
otherGaps.evidence: index.tsx Trigger (lines 54-85) wraps children in <span className="text-shimmer"> and appends only Disclosure.Indicator; no leading icon slot and no streaming-driven step indicator exist; chain-of-thought.css only styles .chain-of-thought--streaming .chain-of-thought__trigger color.
otherGaps.suggestedFix: Optionally render a leading status icon/spinner in the Trigger when isStreaming, and let Steps/Step react to streaming to show the active step indicator (animated dot) per HeroUI's streaming agent-trace example.

otherGaps.title: Display type taken only from explicit `kind` prop — no mediaType/MIME inference
otherGaps.detail: HeroUI Pro infers the preview type from a `mediaType` prop (audio/document/image/video/unknown) or derives it from the file MIME type. Vela's API exposes only `kind?: 'image' | 'video' | 'file'` (index.tsx:4-8) with no MIME/mediaType inference and no audio handling, so a real File/blob attachment must be manually typed by the caller.
otherGaps.evidence: ChatAttachmentProps: `kind?: ChatAttachmentKind` where ChatAttachmentKind = 'image' | 'video' | 'file' (index.tsx:4-8); preview branch keys solely off `kind` (index.tsx:18-26).
otherGaps.suggestedFix: Accept a `mediaType`/`type` (MIME) prop and derive the preview kind (including audio) from it when `kind` is not explicitly provided.

otherGaps.title: ScrollButton is disabled (not hidden) at bottom, diverging from HeroUI's appear-only semantics
otherGaps.detail: HeroUI documents the ScrollButton as appearing only when scrolled away from the bottom; the Vela button instead always renders and is set isDisabled={isAtBottom}, relying on the container's CSS visibility:hidden to hide it. A disabled <button> still sits in the a11y tree as a disabled control and as a disabled child of Tooltip.Trigger, which is a different semantic than 'not present', and can interfere with the Tooltip trigger's focus/hover behavior when disabled.
otherGaps.evidence: src/components/chat-conversation/index.tsx line 402 isDisabled={isAtBottom}; container at line 413 data-state={isAtBottom ? 'hidden' : 'visible'}; CSS hides via visibility:hidden + pointer-events:none (src/styles/components/chat-conversation.css line 45-48).
otherGaps.suggestedFix: Stop forcing isDisabled from isAtBottom (let the container's hidden state and pointer-events handle inertness), or conditionally render the button only when !isAtBottom, so the at-bottom state matches HeroUI's 'button does not appear' contract and avoids a disabled control inside Tooltip.Trigger.

otherGaps.title: Expand/collapse has no enter/exit animation — `hidden` attribute defeats any height/opacity transition
otherGaps.detail: HeroUI Pro's collapsible tool card animates the body open/closed. Vela sets `hidden={!expanded}` on the content node (index.tsx:129), which removes it from layout entirely, so the collapsed CSS state (`opacity:0; height:0`) can never transition — toggling is an instant snap with no measured-height animation.
otherGaps.evidence: src/components/chat-tool/index.tsx:125-131 sets `hidden={!expanded}` and `aria-hidden={!expanded}` on `.chat-tool__content`; chat-tool.css:128-132 defines only a static collapsed state (`opacity:0; height:0 !important`) with no transition declared.
otherGaps.suggestedFix: Keep the content mounted (drop the `hidden` attribute, rely on a measured max-height/height transition and inert/visibility for a11y), or drive enter/exit with a real animation (Motion/CSS transition on measured height) so expand-collapse animates like the reference.

otherGaps.title: Links lack target/rel security attributes
otherGaps.detail: Rendered anchors get only href (and optional title); there is no target=_blank handling and no rel=noopener noreferrer, so external links opened from AI markdown have no tab-nabbing protection, diverging from safe AI link rendering.
otherGaps.evidence: link nodes built at index.tsx:152-167 set only key/href/title; grep for target/rel/noopener in index.tsx returned nothing.
otherGaps.suggestedFix: In the anchor branch of parseInline (index.tsx ~152), add target="_blank" and rel="noopener noreferrer" (or noreferrer) for external (http/https) hrefs in src/components/markdown/index.tsx.

otherGaps.title: No nested lists, loose lists, or autolinks (GFM gaps)
otherGaps.detail: The parser requires every line of a block to match a single flat list marker, so an indented child item breaks list detection; there is also no autolink (<url>/bare url) or multi-paragraph list-item support that remark-gfm provides.
otherGaps.evidence: list detection uses lines.every(...) against a flat marker (index.tsx:402 and 411); list items are rendered one-line-per-li in renderListItem (index.tsx:341-365) with no recursion into child blocks.
otherGaps.suggestedFix: Either adopt react-markdown+remark-gfm as documented, or extend the parser in src/components/markdown/index.tsx to recurse on indented child lines and recognize autolinks.

otherGaps.title: 'submitted' state renders a loading Spinner but behaves and labels as the Stop button
otherGaps.detail: isStoppable lumps 'submitted' with 'streaming', so during the just-sent loading phase the button shows a Spinner yet has aria-label 'Stop generating' and pressing it calls stop(), conflating a loading indicator with a stop affordance.
otherGaps.evidence: const isStoppable = status === 'submitted' || status === 'streaming' (index.tsx:508); for submitted defaultIcon is <Spinner size="sm"/> (:524-526); aria-label = isStoppable ? 'Stop generating' : 'Send message' (:545) and handlePress calls stop() when isStoppable (:513-517).
otherGaps.suggestedFix: Either keep 'submitted' non-interactive with a loading-specific aria-label (e.g. 'Sending') and only treat 'streaming' as stoppable, or show the StopIcon in 'submitted' too — but do not show a passive Spinner while wiring it as the stop control.

otherGaps.title: locale-options ignores nearest I18nProvider (no useLocale integration)
otherGaps.detail: HeroUI Pro documents that `locale` 'overrides the locale from the nearest I18nProvider'; this implementation only reads the explicit `locale` prop and falls back to the JS runtime default, so inside a react-aria I18nProvider it silently formats with the wrong locale instead of inheriting the provider's locale.
otherGaps.evidence: index.tsx:28 `new Intl.NumberFormat(locale, formatOptions)` passes `locale` (undefined when prop omitted) with no useLocale()/I18nProvider lookup; grep shows the component imports only `forwardRef`/`clsx` and never `react-aria-components` or `useLocale`, unlike sibling number-field.
otherGaps.suggestedFix: Import `useLocale` (or `useNumberFormatter`) from react-aria-components and use `locale ?? localeFromContext` so the value inherits the nearest I18nProvider locale when no explicit prop is given.

otherGaps.title: HoldConfirm is pointer-only; keyboard users cannot trigger hold-to-confirm
otherGaps.detail: HoldConfirm starts/cancels its hold timer solely from pointerdown/pointerup/pointerleave/pointercancel on the host, so a keyboard user holding Space/Enter on the focused RAC button never starts the sweep or fires onComplete. The same keyboard-press contract that the Highlight satisfies (via RAC data-pressed) is unmet here.
otherGaps.evidence: index.tsx:259-262 registers host pointerdown/pointerup/pointerleave/pointercancel only; handleDown (238) and handleRelease (250) have no key-event path, so onComplete (246) can never fire from the keyboard.
otherGaps.suggestedFix: Observe the host's pressed state (RAC sets data-pressed on Space/Enter) via a MutationObserver or add keydown(start)/keyup(cancel) listeners for Space/Enter so holding the key drives the same setIsHolding/timer flow as a pointer hold.

otherGaps.title: Undo/redo buttons have no enabled/disabled state from history
otherGaps.detail: The undo and redo ActionButtons always run execCommand but never reflect whether undo/redo is available; ActionButton only disables on isDisabled. A parity editor greys out undo/redo when the history stack is empty.
otherGaps.evidence: src/components/rich-text-editor/index.tsx:490-514 ActionButton `disabled={editor.isDisabled}` only; activeCommands tracking (lines 305-318) excludes undo/redo and there is no queryCommandEnabled usage (grep returned none).
otherGaps.suggestedFix: Track document.queryCommandEnabled('undo'/'redo') in handleSelectionChange/handleInput and disable the corresponding buttons when history is empty.

otherGaps.title: Missing `isModal` prop (HeroUI default true) to render non-modal sheet
otherGaps.detail: HeroUI documents `isModal` (default true) that toggles whether the sheet renders as a modal overlay (non-modal = no backdrop scrim / page remains interactive). The Vela Sheet exposes no such prop; it always renders the modal Drawer/ModalOverlay path.
otherGaps.evidence: SheetProps (index.tsx:48-68) has no isModal; SheetRoot/NestedRoot (index.tsx:513,562) always render `<Drawer ...>` with the modal ModalOverlay base, and grep for `isModal` in index.tsx returns nothing.
otherGaps.suggestedFix: Add an `isModal?: boolean` prop and forward it to the underlying Drawer/ModalOverlay (RAC supports `isDismissable`/non-modal modes) so consumers can opt out of the modal scrim.

otherGaps.title: Missing 'dashed' indicator variant
otherGaps.detail: HeroUI Pro's chart tooltip indicator supports dot, line, and dashed. Vela only models dot and line, so the dashed indicator section cannot be reproduced.
otherGaps.evidence: src/components/chart-tooltip/index.tsx:4 `export type ChartTooltipIndicator = 'dot' | 'line';` and indicator className only ever resolves to chart-tooltip__indicator--dot/--line (line 39).
otherGaps.suggestedFix: Add 'dashed' to ChartTooltipIndicator and render the corresponding chart-tooltip__indicator--dashed class in Item (index.tsx:38-42), and thread it through LineChart.TooltipContent's indicator prop.

otherGaps.title: event-create requires a drag; a single click on empty grid creates nothing
otherGaps.detail: onEventCreate only fires when movedRef became true (pointer moved >4px or crossed a snap minute). A plain click on an empty slot — a common create gesture — produces a transient preview that is discarded on pointerup, so click-to-create is unsupported.
otherGaps.evidence: handlePointerUp (L1184): 'if (movedRef.current && draft) onEventCreate(draft)'; movedRef starts false (L1157) and only flips on movement (L1169-1172).
otherGaps.suggestedFix: On pointerup with no movement, still emit a default-duration create (e.g. the snapped slot start + slotDuration) so a single click on the empty grid creates an event, matching typical calendar create UX.

otherGaps.title: Anchor navigation does not move keyboard focus to the target section
otherGaps.detail: Clicking an Item smooth-scrolls and sets active (scrollToItem, lines 476-498) but never focuses the destination, so keyboard/AT users are scrolled visually yet their focus/reading position stays on the closed popover. HeroUI Pro TOC links move the user to the section. The target heading is also not made focusable (no tabindex management).
otherGaps.evidence: scrollToItem calls target.scrollIntoView / root.scrollTo only (index.tsx:488-495); there is no target.focus() or focus management after navigation.
otherGaps.suggestedFix: After scrolling, move focus to the target section (e.g. set tabindex=-1 on the target if needed and call target.focus({preventScroll:true})) so keyboard navigation lands on the section.

otherGaps.title: focusing a natively-focusable child (e.g. inner button/anchor) does not open the card
otherGaps.detail: The focus-visible open handler lives only on the wrapper <span>. When the child is natively focusable, resolvedTabIndex is undefined so the span is not in the tab order and focus lands on the inner element; the span's onFocus still fires via bubbling, but the demos and contract expect focus on the actual interactive trigger to open the card — this works only because React focus events bubble, and breaks if a consumer stops propagation. The focus-trigger contract is fragile for the recommended (natively-focusable child) usage shown in the a11y test.
otherGaps.evidence: Trigger sets resolvedTabIndex = tabIndex ?? (hasFocusableChild(children) ? undefined : 0) (line 113); handleFocus is bound to the span only (lines 133-136, 158), relying on React's synthetic focus bubbling from the inner button used in the a11y test (index.test.tsx lines 104-113).
otherGaps.suggestedFix: This is acceptable via React focus bubbling, but consider documenting/asserting it; if a consumer's child calls stopPropagation on focus the card silently won't open — a test exercising focus on an inner button (not the wrapper) would lock the behavior.

otherGaps.title: Nested 'Agent Trace' variants not demonstrated/supported
otherGaps.detail: HeroUI documents 4 sections including 'Agent Trace' and 'Agent Trace Streaming' (multiple collapsible reasoning blocks / nested steps). The Vela demo shows only basic, controlled, and streaming (3 root instances) with no nested ChainOfThought composition, so the nested-trace variants are unverified.
otherGaps.evidence: ai-overlay-demos.tsx ChainOfThoughtDemo (lines 312-352) contains exactly 3 <ChainOfThought> roots (defaultExpanded / isExpanded / isStreaming) and no ChainOfThought nested inside a Step; WebFetch of the docs lists 'Agent Trace' and 'Agent Trace Streaming' as variant sections.
otherGaps.suggestedFix: Add a demo composing nested ChainOfThought/Disclosure blocks inside steps to exercise the agent-trace variant, and confirm the wrapper renders correctly when nested.

otherGaps.title: No blob: URL lifecycle handling for previews
otherGaps.detail: HeroUI Pro explicitly revokes each blob: URL when an attachment is removed to avoid leaks for uploaded-file previews. Vela's ChatAttachment just renders /
otherGaps.evidence: onRemove handler is a plain `onClick={onRemove}` (index.tsx:35) and `src` is rendered directly with no cleanup effect; no useEffect/revokeObjectURL anywhere.
otherGaps.suggestedFix: When a preview src is an object URL, revoke it via URL.revokeObjectURL in a cleanup effect on unmount, and/or expose a helper so removal frees the blob.

otherGaps.title: No custom-trigger slot — 'composable' variant cannot customize the header layout
otherGaps.detail: HeroUI Pro documents a composable variant offering 'slots for custom trigger and content layouts.' Vela's trigger DOM is hardcoded to `label + status indicator` (index.tsx:82-90) with no way to supply a custom trigger node/render prop. The composable demo (ai-overlay-demos.tsx:2162-2177) just nests a default ChatTool inside a ChatMessage, which composes the message — not the tool's trigger/content slots.
otherGaps.evidence: src/components/chat-tool/index.tsx:82-90 builds `triggerLabel` from `label`+`statusLabel`+`statusIcon` only; ChatToolProps (index.tsx:22-32) exposes no `trigger`/`renderTrigger` slot.
otherGaps.suggestedFix: Add an optional custom-trigger slot (e.g. a `trigger` render prop or a `ChatTool.Trigger` subcomponent) so consumers can fully control the header layout while preserving the button/aria-expanded/aria-controls wiring.

otherGaps.title: Cannot send a prompt that has attachments but no text
otherGaps.detail: Both submit() and the Send disabled-logic gate purely on trimmed text length, so an attachments-only message can never be submitted; the root has no awareness of attachment count.
otherGaps.evidence: submit() returns early when value.trim().length === 0 (index.tsx:905); Send isDisabled includes (!isStoppable && value.trim().length === 0) (:547); grep shows no attachmentCount/hasAttachments/allowEmptySubmit anywhere.
otherGaps.suggestedFix: Let the root accept a canSubmit/hasAttachments signal (or an allowEmptySubmit prop) so submit() and the Send enabled-state can permit attachment-only prompts, matching the reference where attachments alone are submittable.

otherGaps.title: No first-class selection callback (onSelect / value) at Items or Root level
otherGaps.detail: HeroUI Pro frames this as a composable prompt-starter where the parent reacts to a chosen suggestion. The Vela implementation exposes only per-Item native onClick; there is no onSelect/value prop on Items or Root, so every consumer must hand-wire onClick on each Item/CardItem and manage selection state manually (as the demos do at ai-overlay-demos.tsx:651, 670, 2583).
otherGaps.evidence: src/components/prompt-suggestion/index.tsx:46-48 PromptSuggestionItemsProps only adds `variant`; Item (57-73) and CardItem (75-102) only spread native ButtonHTMLAttributes — no onSelect/value/onAction anywhere.
otherGaps.suggestedFix: Add an optional onSelect/value (or onAction) prop on Items that wires each child Item's click to report its value, giving a real controlled selection API instead of forcing manual per-item onClick wiring.

otherGaps.title: Handle click neither closes the sheet (no snap points) nor supports `preventCycle`
otherGaps.detail: HeroUI docs: clicking Sheet.Handle should "cycle through snap points or close the sheet," gated by a `preventCycle` prop. Here clicking the handle only cycles when snapsEnabled; with no snap points the click is a no-op (it does not close), and there is no `preventCycle` prop to disable cycling.
otherGaps.evidence: index.tsx:481-484 handleClick calls `dragContext?.cycleSnapPoint?.()`, and cycleSnapPoint returns early `if (!snapsEnabled)` (index.tsx:340-343); SheetHandleProps (index.tsx:110-116) defines no preventCycle.
otherGaps.suggestedFix: Add a `preventCycle` prop on Handle; when snap points are absent and cycling is allowed, fall back to closing the sheet on click, matching the documented behavior.

otherGaps.title: all-day events cannot be moved or resized by drag
otherGaps.detail: AllDayEvent supports only click-select and keyboard delete; there is no drag-to-reschedule across days or edge-resize to extend the multi-day span, so the all-day-events contract is read/select-only for pointer users.
otherGaps.evidence: AllDayEvent (L951-992) renders a <button> with onClick=setSelectedEventId and handleKeyDown only; it never references onEventMove/onEventResize and renders no resize handle.
otherGaps.suggestedFix: Wire pointer drag on AllDayEvent (horizontal -> colStart change -> onEventMove) and edge handles for span resize in index.tsx, consistent with the timed-event interaction model.

otherGaps.title: Calendar grid lacks grid/gridcell/columnheader semantics and per-event time labels
otherGaps.detail: The week/day/month structure is plain divs; the only ARIA role is role="button" on the timed Event. Day headers are not columnheaders, day columns/month cells are not gridcells, and events expose only their title text (no aria-label including the start–end time), so screen-reader navigation and announcement diverge from an accessible calendar grid.
otherGaps.evidence: grep for role= in index.tsx returns a single hit: role="button" at L1540; WeekHeader day headers (L796-817), DayColumn (L1219) and MonthCell (L1746) have no role; Event has no aria-label (only renders title + time spans as text, L1561-1566).
otherGaps.suggestedFix: In index.tsx add a grid/row/gridcell + columnheader role layer (or aria-labels) and give each event an aria-label combining title and formatted start–end time so assistive tech announces full context.

otherGaps.title: Items group has no list semantics (no role="group"/"list" or accessible label)
otherGaps.detail: The items container is a bare <div> with only a variant class; the Group's label renders as a plain styled div with no programmatic association, so screen readers get a flat run of buttons with no grouping or count. HeroUI Pro presents these as a labeled set of suggestion choices.
otherGaps.evidence: src/components/prompt-suggestion/index.tsx:50-54 Items renders `<div className={...variant} {...rest}/>` with no role; Group label at lines 36 is a div with no id wired to aria-labelledby.
otherGaps.suggestedFix: Give Items role="group" (or list) and associate the Group label via aria-labelledby/aria-label so the suggestion set is announced as a labeled group.

otherGaps.title: No keyboard path to move/resize events (drag/resize are pointer-only)
otherGaps.detail: Event keyboard handlers only support Enter/Space (select) and Delete/Backspace; there are no Arrow-key handlers to nudge start/end times or move days, so the event-drag/event-resize contract is inaccessible to keyboard users.
otherGaps.evidence: grep for ArrowUp/ArrowDown/ArrowLeft/ArrowRight in index.tsx returns no matches; handleKeyDown (L1520-1535) handles only Enter/' '/Delete/Backspace.
otherGaps.suggestedFix: Add Arrow-key handling on the focused Event (e.g. Up/Down to shift start by slot/snap calling onEventMove; Shift+Arrow to resize calling onEventResize) so drag/resize have a keyboard equivalent.

otherGaps.title: Test coverage is axe-only; no formatter/indicator behavior asserted
otherGaps.detail: The only test renders a static tooltip and runs axe. It does not assert that indicator color is applied, that dot vs line classes render, or that the formatter/labelFormatter auto-content path produces correct output.
otherGaps.evidence: src/components/chart-tooltip/index.test.tsx:6-27 contains a single `has no axe a11y violations` test and nothing for indicatorColor style, indicator variant class, or LineChart.TooltipContent formatter.
otherGaps.suggestedFix: Add tests asserting indicatorColor maps to inline backgroundColor, the --dot/--line indicator class is applied, and (against LineChart.TooltipContent) that formatter/labelFormatter transform values, hideLabel hides the header.

otherGaps.title: Test coverage only runs axe; no interaction/popover assertions
otherGaps.detail: The single test renders the two components and runs axe; it asserts nothing about the open-feedback, the (absent) popover, keyboard activation, or onOpen, so none of the behavioral contract is regression-guarded.
otherGaps.evidence: index.test.tsx (entire file, lines 6-20) contains one `it('has no axe a11y violations')` test; no userEvent, no fireEvent, no assertion on data-opened, onOpen, or any popover role.
otherGaps.suggestedFix: Add interaction tests once the popover is real: hover/focus opens HoverCard.Content with title/description, Esc/blur closes it, and onOpen fires on click.

otherGaps.title: Tests cover only static axe render — no tooltip/formatter/mixed-series behavior asserted
otherGaps.detail: index.test.tsx has a single case that renders the chart and runs axe; it never simulates hover, never asserts TooltipContent output (label/value/indicator/formatter), and never verifies Area+Bar+Line co-render. The tooltip-hover and mixed-series contracts are therefore unverified by the suite (jsdom layout limits are real, but the TooltipContent pure component could be unit-tested directly with synthetic payload).
otherGaps.evidence: index.test.tsx:22-37 only `expect(await axe(container)).toHaveNoViolations()`; no fireEvent/hover and no assertion on ChartTooltip.Item rendering or `formatter`.
otherGaps.suggestedFix: Add a unit test that renders <ComposedChart.TooltipContent active payload={...} formatter={...} /> and asserts the header, item labels/values, indicator color, hideLabel and formatter behavior independent of layout.

otherGaps.title: Tests cover only axe smoke, not tooltip/active/legend behavior
otherGaps.detail: index.test.tsx contains a single axe-violations test that renders a Pie + Tooltip but asserts nothing about tooltip-hover output, active-slice, or legend. None of the four contract tokens are behaviorally regression-locked.
otherGaps.evidence: index.test.tsx has one it() block ('has no axe a11y violations', lines 24-32); grep for 'fireEvent|mouseEnter|getByRole|activeShape|legend' in the test returns nothing.
otherGaps.suggestedFix: Add tests that render TooltipContent with an active payload and assert the chart-tooltip structure, plus active-slice/legend interaction coverage once those are wired.

otherGaps.title: Test suite only runs axe; tooltip-hover and progress-arc behavior are untested
otherGaps.detail: index.test.tsx contains a single test asserting no axe violations and never renders/asserts the tooltip, hover behavior, or arc cells, so none of the three contract tokens (responsive-chart, tooltip-hover, progress-arc) have behavioral regression coverage.
otherGaps.evidence: index.test.tsx:23-37 has exactly one it('has no axe a11y violations') and no fireEvent/queries against tooltip or bars.
otherGaps.suggestedFix: Add tests that render with RadialChart.Tooltip + TooltipContent and assert the ChartTooltip role="tooltip" DOM appears on hover, plus a render assertion that the expected number of Cell/bar paths exist.

otherGaps.title: Tests assert nothing about rendering/contract beyond axe
otherGaps.detail: index.test.tsx contains only a single axe a11y assertion; it never verifies the .text-shimmer class, data-slot="text-shimmer", className merge, or children rendering, so the loading-animation/text-rendering/color-variants contract is effectively unguarded by the regression suite.
otherGaps.evidence: index.test.tsx has one `it('has no axe a11y violations')` test rendering <TextShimmer>Thinking…</TextShimmer>; no assertions on class, data-slot, or children.
otherGaps.suggestedFix: Add tests asserting the rendered span carries class 'text-shimmer', preserves a passed className, exposes data-slot="text-shimmer", and renders children — covering the color-variant path via a className/style color too.

otherGaps.title: Dead hover-card slot leaks misleading DOM contract
otherGaps.detail: Because the component emits data-slot="hover-card-trigger" / className="hover-card__trigger" without any HoverCard, consumers and tests targeting hover-card slots get a misleading hook that never opens a popover, and the wrapper span is purely decorative.
otherGaps.evidence: index.tsx line 103: `<span className="hover-card__trigger" data-slot="hover-card-trigger">` with no corresponding hover-card-content anywhere in the tree.
otherGaps.suggestedFix: Either wire the real HoverCard so these slots are meaningful, or remove the orphan hover-card__trigger wrapper so the DOM does not advertise a popover that does not exist.

otherGaps.title: Test coverage exercises only axe; no copy/feedback/highlight assertions
otherGaps.detail: The single test renders the component and runs axe. None of the contract behaviors (clipboard write on press, icon/aria-label swap to Copied, 2s reset, language highlighting, overflow scrolling) are covered, so regressions in copy-action would go undetected.
otherGaps.evidence: index.test.tsx:6-19 contains one `it('has no axe a11y violations')` and no other cases; no userEvent/clipboard mock, no assertions on data-copy-status.
otherGaps.suggestedFix: Add tests: click CopyButton with a clipboard mock and assert navigator.clipboard.writeText called, aria-label becomes 'Copied', data-copy-status='copied', and resets after the timer (fake timers); plus a highlight token assertion.

otherGaps.title: Controlled custom-value rendering not covered by any test
otherGaps.detail: index.test.tsx covers uncontrolled selection via onSelectionChange and disabled, but never asserts CellSelect.Value's render-prop output nor a controlled value→display sync, so the custom-value contract has zero regression coverage even at the API level.
otherGaps.evidence: index.test.tsx:81-91 tests `onSelectionChange` click only; renderCell (line 13-32) always uses `<CellSelect.Value />` with no children; no test reads the trigger's displayed value text.
otherGaps.suggestedFix: Add a test that renders CellSelect.Value with a function child and asserts the custom node renders for the selected item (and the placeholder branch when empty).

otherGaps.title: Unit test exercises the deprecated onSelectionChange API instead of value/onChange
otherGaps.detail: The component correctly forwards the modern RAC controlled API (the demo at forms-demos.tsx:470 uses value/onChange), but the test asserts against the deprecated alias. react-stately useSelectState SelectProps marks selectedKey/onSelectionChange as @deprecated in favor of ValueBase value/onChange, so the test validates a legacy code path the canonical contract no longer recommends.
otherGaps.evidence: index.test.tsx:68 renderInline({ onSelectionChange }) and index.test.tsx:74 expect(onSelectionChange).toHaveBeenCalledWith('updated'); contrast with node_modules react-stately useSelectState.d.ts where onSelectionChange is annotated @deprecated and SelectProps extends ValueBase<ValueType<M>, ChangeValueType<M>> (value/onChange).
otherGaps.suggestedFix: No change needed in src/components/inline-select/index.tsx (it already passes through both). Optionally update the test to assert the non-deprecated onChange handler to match the demo and the documented controlled-value contract.

otherGaps.title: Stacking/accessibility chart props are not overridable
otherGaps.detail: AreaChartProps exposes no accessibilityLayer, syncId, stackOffset, reverseStackOrder, or throttleDelay. Keyboard a11y still works because recharts 3.x defaults accessibilityLayer to true, but consumers cannot opt out or tune stacking/sync behavior, diverging from the bar-chart API which forwards all of these.
otherGaps.evidence: AreaChartProps in index.tsx:25-38 only declares className/style/data/height/width/margin/children. recharts default accessibilityLayer:true confirmed at node_modules/recharts/es6/state/rootPropsSlice.js:9 and CartesianChart.js:24. bar-chart/index.tsx:34-65 Picks accessibilityLayer/syncId/syncMethod/stackOffset/reverseStackOrder/throttleDelay/baseValue.
otherGaps.suggestedFix: Extend AreaChartProps to Pick the same recharts chart-level prop set bar-chart uses (accessibilityLayer, syncId, syncMethod, stackOffset, reverseStackOrder, throttleDelay, baseValue) and forward them to <RechartsAreaChart>.

otherGaps.title: prefers-reduced-motion only disables dots/pulse, not skeleton
otherGaps.detail: The reduced-motion guard covers only the animated dot/pulse elements; the skeleton has nothing to disable today, but once a skeleton loading-animation is added it must also be covered to avoid a reduced-motion regression.
otherGaps.evidence: chat-loader.css lines 117-123: `@media (prefers-reduced-motion:reduce) { .chat-loader__dot, .chat-loader__pulse { animation: none } }` — no selector for .chat-loader__skeleton-line/avatar.
otherGaps.suggestedFix: When adding the skeleton shimmer, extend the prefers-reduced-motion:reduce block to also neutralize the skeleton primitives' animation.

otherGaps.title: Multi-select behavior not actually asserted (only aria-multiselectable attribute checked)
otherGaps.detail: The multi-select test only checks the listbox has aria-multiselectable='true' but never selects a second item to confirm both stay selected and the popover does not close (single mode closes-on-select, multiple does not). Behavior is correct at runtime via RAC defaults, but the documented multi-select variant's distinguishing behavior is unverified by tests.
otherGaps.evidence: index.test.tsx:77-84 only does expect(listbox).toHaveAttribute('aria-multiselectable','true') and never clicks two options; the component sets no shouldCloseOnSelect override so RAC's mode-dependent default governs it.
otherGaps.suggestedFix: This is a test-coverage gap, not a component defect; src/components/inline-select/index.tsx needs no change. Add a test that toggles two options in selectionMode='multiple' and asserts both retain aria-selected and the listbox stays open.

otherGaps.title: 300ms unmount delay is decoupled from animation duration and from a custom className's animation
otherGaps.detail: EXIT_FALLBACK_MS is hardcoded to 300ms while the built-in exit animation is duration-200; because the timer is the only unmount path (see prior finding), a consumer who passes a custom className with a longer exit animation will have the DOM ripped out at 300ms mid-animation, and a shorter one leaves stale DOM ~100ms after it finishes. There is no prop to tune this and no read of the wrapper's computed animation-duration.
otherGaps.evidence: index.tsx:23 `const EXIT_FALLBACK_MS = 300;` and index.tsx:48 `setTimeout(() => setIsPresent(false), EXIT_FALLBACK_MS)`; className prop is forwarded to the animated wrapper at line 71.
otherGaps.suggestedFix: Tie unmount to the actual animationend (fixing the handler) and keep the timer only as a true fallback, or expose the exit duration so the fallback can match a custom animation.

otherGaps.title: Test file covers only axe, no behavioral assertions
otherGaps.detail: src/components/bar-chart/index.test.tsx contains a single test asserting axe has no violations; it never exercises tooltip-hover, layout='vertical' horizontal orientation, or stacked-series (stackId) behavior. This is a coverage gap, not a contract gap — the component itself implements all four tokens. Per project memory, recharts renders empty in jsdom (no layout), so behavioral assertions are genuinely hard here.
otherGaps.evidence: index.test.tsx has exactly one `it('has no axe a11y violations')`; no render asserts on chart-tooltip DOM, stack geometry, or vertical layout. Test run output: 'Tests 1 passed (1)'.
otherGaps.suggestedFix: No runtime/source fix required for parity. Optionally add light DOM-shape assertions (e.g. that BarChart.Bar with stackId renders, that layout='vertical' is threaded to RechartsBarChart) where jsdom permits; do not edit index.tsx for this.

otherGaps.title: Radar tooltip leaves recharts default polar cursor enabled, unlike sibling radial demo
otherGaps.detail: Both radar demos pass `content` to RadarChart.Tooltip but not `cursor={false}`, so recharts draws its default radial cursor line on hover. The sibling RadialChart demo explicitly sets `cursor={false}` (charts-demos.tsx line 187), making the radar hover affordance inconsistent with the rest of the chart family and with HeroUI Pro's clean radar tooltip. Note this is demo-level wiring; the library correctly forwards the prop.
otherGaps.evidence: charts-demos.tsx line 219 `<RadarChart.Tooltip content={<RadarChart.TooltipContent />} />` and line 1377 (no cursor prop) vs line 187 `<RadialChart.Tooltip cursor={false} ...>`.
otherGaps.suggestedFix: Either pass `cursor={false}` in the radar demos, or have ChartTooltip wrapper default the recharts Tooltip cursor to a polar-appropriate value so radar hover matches the reference.

otherGaps.title: Expand/collapse height animation is silently disabled whenever drag-and-drop is enabled
otherGaps.detail: FileTreeRoot (index.tsx:443) computes `useMotionSections = !reduceMotion && rest.dragAndDropHooks === undefined`, so any tree with dragAndDropHooks falls back to DefaultCollectionRenderer and loses the Motion expand/collapse transition entirely — even when reduceMotion is false. This is a deliberate, correct trade-off (the custom MotionCollectionRoot ignores renderDropIndicator at line 400, which would break RAC drop indicators), but it means the drag-and-drop variant diverges from the animated expand/collapse shown in the other variants.
otherGaps.evidence: Line 443: `const useMotionSections = !reduceMotion && rest.dragAndDropHooks === undefined;` and line 400 destructures `renderDropIndicator: _renderDropIndicator` (ignored). RAC Tree.mjs:382 passes renderDropIndicator into CollectionRoot, confirming the custom root would drop indicators if used during DnD.
otherGaps.suggestedFix: Optionally thread renderDropIndicator through MotionCollectionRoot's per-node rendering so motion sections and drop indicators can coexist, enabling animated expand/collapse in the drag-and-drop variant; otherwise document this as an intentional limitation.

otherGaps.title: ListView.LoadMore is a styled div, not RAC's load-more sentinel
otherGaps.detail: ListView.LoadMore (index.tsx:170-178) is a plain <div data-slot="list-view-load-more"> with no connection to RAC's GridListLoadMoreItem sentinel (GridList.d.ts:142) or onLoadMore/isLoading infinite-scroll wiring, so it provides no real pagination behavior. This is low severity because HeroUI Pro's list-view docs do not document a loading/load-more variant, so it is not a parity contract item — but the exported primitive is inert/misleading.
otherGaps.evidence: index.tsx:170-178 LoadMore is a forwardRef div only; RAC exposes GridListLoadMoreItemProps with onLoadMore-style sentinel behavior at GridList.d.ts:142, which is not used.
otherGaps.suggestedFix: Either back ListView.LoadMore with RAC's GridListLoadMoreItem sentinel (wiring isLoading/onLoadMore) or remove the inert subcomponent to avoid implying functionality the library does not provide.

otherGaps.title: Root variant API avatar path bypasses MessageAvatar spacer logic
otherGaps.detail: ChatMessageRoot's assistant branch wraps a provided avatar in a separate inline AvatarSlot component (a plain div with class chat-message__avatar) rather than reusing the MessageAvatar subcomponent, so the show/spacer/Avatar-wrapping behavior diverges between the legacy variant API and the compound API.
otherGaps.evidence: index.tsx:128-132 defines a duplicate `const AvatarSlot = ({children}) => <div data-slot="chat-message-avatar" className="chat-message__avatar">` used at index.tsx:150, while the public MessageAvatar (index.tsx:46-75) carries the show=false spacer + real Avatar rendering used at index.tsx:152.
otherGaps.suggestedFix: Route the variant API's avatar through the same MessageAvatar subcomponent (e.g. <MessageAvatar show>{avatar}</MessageAvatar> or render children inside it) so both APIs share one avatar-slot implementation and behavior.

otherGaps.title: theme prop is an inert pass-through
otherGaps.detail: CodeBlock.Code accepts a `theme` prop (default 'github-light') matching HeroUI's Shiki API, but it is only emitted as data-theme on <code> and the runtime CSS never keys off it; dark mode is driven exclusively by ancestor theme selectors. Passing theme="github-dark" in a light context has no effect.
otherGaps.evidence: index.tsx:288 `<code data-language={normalizedLanguage} data-theme={theme}>`; comment line 25 admits '仅保留 API，暗色由 CSS 主题选择器接管'. No `code[data-theme=...]` rule exists in runtime styles.css.
otherGaps.suggestedFix: Either honor the theme prop (e.g. apply per-instance token colors via the data-theme value) or drop it from the public type so the API does not advertise a no-op control.

otherGaps.title: no hover affordance even in the interactive demo
otherGaps.detail: The primary interactive demo labels itself '受控（点击或方向键打分，按下星星有缩放反馈）' — it advertises press scaling feedback but no hover preview, matching the implementation's lack of hover. Reviewers comparing to HeroUI Pro will see stars that do not light up on mouse-over before clicking.
otherGaps.evidence: src/showcase/demos/feedback-nav-demos.tsx line 77 demo label mentions only press feedback; the controlled Rating (lines 78-82) has no hover-related props, consistent with index.tsx having no hover wiring.
otherGaps.suggestedFix: Once hover-preview is implemented in src/components/rating/index.tsx, the existing interactive demos will exercise it automatically; update the demo label to reflect hover preview.

otherGaps.title: Documented root `storage` prop for custom/SSR persistence is not exposed
otherGaps.detail: HeroUI Pro documents a root-level custom storage option (PanelGroupStorage / SSR cookie persistence) alongside autoSaveId. Vela hardcodes window.localStorage with prefix 'react-resizable-panels:' and offers no way to inject a custom storage layer (cookies, sessionStorage, server-provided), so the documented custom/SSR persistence path is unavailable.
otherGaps.evidence: src/components/resizable/index.tsx readStoredLayout/writeStoredLayout (lines 149-169) call window.localStorage directly with STORAGE_PREFIX; ResizableProps (lines 42-57) exposes only autoSaveId, no `storage`. Engine offers useDefaultLayout({ storage }: LayoutStorage) per d.ts lines 412-432.
otherGaps.suggestedFix: Add an optional `storage?: { getItem; setItem }` prop to ResizableProps and route readStoredLayout/writeStoredLayout through it (defaulting to window.localStorage), enabling cookie/SSR persistence.

otherGaps.title: Test suite covers none of the contract interactions (snap, drag, nested, placement, detached, backdrop)
otherGaps.detail: The 6 sheet tests only assert single-button trigger, open/close, controlled isOpen, data-slots, and axe. None exercise snap-point stepping, drag-to-dismiss, nested-open parent scaling, placement classes beyond bottom, isDetached, or backdrop variants, so regressions in the contract behaviors are unguarded.
otherGaps.evidence: index.test.tsx:50-107 contains only 6 `it()` blocks; grep for snap/handle/drag/nested/placement/backdrop in the test file returns no matches.
otherGaps.suggestedFix: Add JS-assertable tests (e.g. snap index state via setSnapPoint callbacks, data-sheet-snap-index attribute changes, data-sheet-nested-open on parent) that don't require jsdom layout.

otherGaps.title: accessibilityLayer passthrough is undefined-by-default (relies on recharts resolving back to true)
otherGaps.detail: The wrapper destructures `accessibilityLayer` and forwards it inside chartProps even when the caller omits it, so the value passed to RechartsBarChart is `undefined`. Keyboard navigation (role='application', tabIndex=0 on the surface, arrow-key tooltip) still works because recharts' resolveDefaultProps treats undefined as 'use default' (default true), so this is harmless today but is an implicit dependency on recharts internals rather than an explicit guarantee.
otherGaps.evidence: index.tsx:151/191 destructures and re-emits `accessibilityLayer` with no default; resolveDefaultProps.js:45 `if (acc[key] === undefined && dp[key] !== undefined)` and CartesianChart.js:24 `accessibilityLayer: true` restore the default; RootSurface.js:47/52 apply tabIndex 0 and role 'application' only when the layer is active.
otherGaps.suggestedFix: In src/components/bar-chart/index.tsx default `accessibilityLayer` to `true` in the destructure (`accessibilityLayer = true`) to make keyboard a11y an explicit, recharts-internal-independent guarantee.

otherGaps.title: No accessible name/role on the chart container for screen readers
otherGaps.detail: RadarChartRoot renders a plain div with data-slot='radar-chart' and no role='img'/'figure' or aria-label, so the SVG chart is announced as nothing meaningful. HeroUI Pro charts are otherwise inert SVG; an accessible label is the standard mitigation. The tooltip itself does correctly carry role='tooltip' via ChartTooltipRoot.
otherGaps.evidence: index.tsx lines 42-48: container is `<div className={clsx('radar-chart', className)} data-slot="radar-chart" ...>` with no role or aria-label; rest is spread but no default a11y attributes.
otherGaps.suggestedFix: Allow/forward an aria-label and consider defaulting role='img' (or 'figure') on the container so the visualization has an accessible name.

otherGaps.title: responsive-labels contract only collapses labels, not buttons; component provides no icon-collapse mechanism
otherGaps.detail: HeroUI's responsive-labels behavior is documented as labels becoming SR-only AND action buttons collapsing to icon-only below 640px. Vela only implements the label half via the `.action-bar__label` SR-only @media rule; the responsive-labels demo renders text-labeled buttons (归档/分配/清除) that keep their text at all widths, and ActionBar exposes no API or slot to make buttons collapse responsively. The component leaves button collapsing entirely to consumer markup with no support.
otherGaps.evidence: action-bar.css `@media (max-width:639px){ .action-bar__label{ clip-path: inset(50%) ... }}` hides only the label; demo lines 1736-1752 render Buttons with text children and only aria-label, no responsive icon-only switch.
otherGaps.suggestedFix: Document/clarify that button icon-collapse is consumer-driven, or add a responsive-aware Action sub-slot (e.g. label hidden below 640px while icon stays) so the component itself fulfills the documented 'buttons collapse to icon-only' behavior rather than only the label half.

otherGaps.title: Enter/F2-to-edit keyboard activation only works for custom-editor columns, not the default inline path
otherGaps.detail: handleCellKeyDown (Enter/F2 starts editing) and the click-to-edit trigger button are only used on the `isEditable && column.editor` path. The common inline-editable column (no custom editor) is always an open input, so there is no keyboard 'enter edit mode' affordance and no read-mode cell to receive grid keyboard focus, making cell keyboard navigation inconsistent between the two editable variants.
otherGaps.evidence: index.tsx lines 1169-1175 define handleCellKeyDown but the <button> trigger that wires onKeyDown={handleCellKeyDown} (lines 1290-1298) is only reached in the `isEditable` (custom editor) branch; the inline branch (line 1287) renders inlineEditorNode which has no Enter/F2-to-edit handler.
otherGaps.suggestedFix: Unify the editable-cell rendering so both inline and custom-editor columns show a focusable read-mode trigger that enters edit on Enter/F2, giving consistent keyboard activation and grid focus semantics.

otherGaps.title: No polymorphic `as`/`asChild` on slots prevents correct semantics
otherGaps.detail: None of the subcomponents accept an `as` or `asChild` prop (all are fixed forwardRef<HTMLDivElement>, index.tsx:34-65). Because Title/Description are hardcoded divs with no override hook, consumers cannot supply the h3/p semantics the reference provides, compounding the DOM-semantics gaps above.
otherGaps.evidence: index.tsx:10-15 SectionProps = HTMLAttributes<HTMLDivElement>; all slots forwardRef<HTMLDivElement> with no `as` prop.
otherGaps.suggestedFix: Add an `as` prop (or default Title→h3 / Description→p) so the element tag can match HeroUI's documented semantics.

otherGaps.title: No data-state / open indicator on the trigger for styling or testing parity
otherGaps.detail: The trigger span exposes only data-slot="hover-card-trigger" and a static class; there is no data-state="open|closed" (or data-open) reflecting isOpen. HeroUI/Radix-style hover cards expose an open data attribute on the trigger for styling and assertions; consumers can't style the active trigger purely from markup.
otherGaps.evidence: Trigger render (lines 152-165) emits className 'hover-card__trigger' and data-slot only; isOpen is in context but never reflected onto the trigger element.
otherGaps.suggestedFix: Read isOpen from context in Trigger and emit data-state={isOpen ? 'open' : 'closed'} (or data-open) on the span so the trigger's open state is queryable/styleable.

otherGaps.title: orientation is a visual-only className with no orientation semantics exposed
otherGaps.detail: `orientation` is applied solely as the `kpi-group--${orientation}` modifier class on the RAC Group; no `aria-orientation` or data-orientation attribute is set, so assistive tech and downstream consumers cannot read the group's orientation. HeroUI treats this as primarily layout, so impact is minor, but the prop has no DOM-observable effect beyond styling.
otherGaps.evidence: index.tsx line 27: `className={clsx('kpi-group', `kpi-group--${orientation}`, className)}` — orientation feeds only the class; the Group receives no `aria-orientation` or `data-orientation` prop.
otherGaps.suggestedFix: Optionally surface `data-orientation={orientation}` on the root Group so the value is queryable/testable in the DOM, consistent with how other Vela components expose state via data-attributes.

otherGaps.title: Timeline.Time exposes dateTime prop but it is never wired or defaulted
otherGaps.detail: TimelineTimeProps redeclares dateTime, but the Time component just spreads rest onto <time>; the demo always passes human strings ('09:18','2m ago') as children with no dateTime, so the <time> element has no machine-readable datetime — a minor semantic divergence from a proper timeline timestamp.
otherGaps.evidence: index.tsx:46-48 declares dateTime on TimelineTimeProps and index.tsx:199-201 renders <time {...rest}> with no handling; the demo (data-display-demos.tsx:3839) renders <Timeline.Time>{item.time}</Timeline.Time> with no dateTime.
otherGaps.suggestedFix: Either drop the redundant dateTime type (it is already part of HTMLAttributes<HTMLTimeElement>) or document/encourage passing dateTime, and update at least one demo item to supply a real ISO dateTime for correct <time> semantics.

otherGaps.title: Pressable legend item has no pressed/selected state wiring
otherGaps.detail: isPressable LegendItem is described as 'suitable for clickable filter/highlight legends' (index.tsx:27), but it only renders a bare button with onClick — there is no aria-pressed / aria-selected / data-selected support, so a toggle-filter legend (a common HeroUI Pro chart pattern) cannot convey selected state to assistive tech without the caller hand-rolling aria attributes.
otherGaps.evidence: index.tsx:97-121 LegendItem passes through rest props only; the demo legends (data-display-demos.tsx:493-497, 3630-3631, 3672-3673) are all non-pressable and static, so the pressable filtering path is never exercised.
otherGaps.suggestedFix: Add an optional isSelected/onSelectionChange (or aria-pressed) prop on LegendItem when isPressable, reflecting it via aria-pressed + data-selected so dimmed/active legend filtering reads correctly to screen readers.

otherGaps.title: Trigger inlines text-shimmer markup instead of reusing TextShimmer component
otherGaps.detail: The Trigger hand-writes <span className="text-shimmer"> rather than composing the library's own TextShimmer component, duplicating the slot/class contract and risking drift if TextShimmer changes.
otherGaps.evidence: index.tsx lines 72-74 render <span className="text-shimmer" data-slot="text-shimmer">; the project ships src/components/text-shimmer/index.tsx which renders the identical class/data-slot.
otherGaps.suggestedFix: Import and render <TextShimmer> in the Trigger instead of the inline span to keep a single source of truth for the shimmer markup.

otherGaps.title: Decorative skeleton primitives not marked aria-hidden
otherGaps.detail: SkeletonAvatar/SkeletonLine/SkeletonBlock are purely visual placeholders inside a role=status wrapper; they carry no aria-hidden, relying solely on being empty. This is benign today but means any future text/content in them would be double-announced.
otherGaps.evidence: src/components/chat-loader/index.tsx lines 81-119: ChatLoaderSkeletonAvatar/Line/Block render bare <span> with className only, no aria-hidden; the wrapper ChatLoaderSkeleton (lines 134-144) provides role=status + aria-label.
otherGaps.suggestedFix: Add aria-hidden="true" to the SkeletonAvatar/SkeletonLine/SkeletonBlock spans so the wrapper's aria-label is the sole announced content.

otherGaps.title: Menu (menu-action) ships only a trigger button; no built-in overlay in the component
otherGaps.detail: ChatMessageActions.Menu is just an icon-only ghost Button with a MenuIcon; it renders no dropdown/menu itself. The actual menu overlay (Dropdown/Menu/Popover with items) lives only in the showcase demo (MessageMoreActionsMenu), so the library export alone does not provide menu-action behavior — it relies on consumers/composition.
otherGaps.evidence: index.tsx:388-394 `Menu` only renders `<Action ...>{children ?? <MenuIcon />}</Action>`. The dropdown wiring is external: ai-overlay-demos.tsx:402-416 wraps it in `<Dropdown><Dropdown.Trigger><ChatMessageActions.Menu>...` + `Dropdown.Menu` items.
otherGaps.suggestedFix: This is acceptable composition (the trigger is the contract token and the demo wires a real Dropdown), but document that Menu is trigger-only, or optionally provide a composed Menu variant so menu-action works out of the box without consumer-supplied overlay.

otherGaps.title: collapse-state derived from a size heuristic instead of engine collapse semantics
otherGaps.detail: onCollapse/onExpand are inferred by comparing asPercentage to collapsedSize + 0.01 rather than from the engine's authoritative collapse state (panelRef.isCollapsed). With a non-zero minSize and collapsedSize=0 this is usually correct, but it can mis-fire/skip if collapsedSize is configured near minSize or if the engine snaps differently, since the engine collapses on size < minSize, not on reaching collapsedSize exactly.
otherGaps.evidence: src/components/resizable/index.tsx handleResize (lines 333-345): `const collapsed = panelSize.asPercentage <= (collapsedSize ?? 0) + 0.01;` then derives onCollapse/onExpand from prevCollapsedRef. Engine onResize only provides {asPercentage,inPixels} (d.ts line 319/339-341); authoritative isCollapsed() exists on PanelImperativeHandle (d.ts line 229).
otherGaps.suggestedFix: Wire an internal panelRef and call its isCollapsed() inside handleResize to drive onCollapse/onExpand, instead of the magic 0.01 percentage threshold.

otherGaps.title: Description text is not programmatically associated with the drop region
otherGaps.detail: DropZone.Description renders a plain <span data-slot="drop-zone-description"> with no aria wiring. RAC DropZone only auto-links slot="label" to aria-labelledby (confirmed: DropZone.mjs reads props['aria-label']/'aria-labelledby' only, no description handling), so the size/type hint (e.g. 'PDF up to 20MB') is visual-only and not announced as the drop region's description.
otherGaps.evidence: index.tsx Description (lines 661-671) uses a bare span with className 'drop-zone__description' and data-slot, no slot="description"/aria-describedby; RAC DropZone.mjs grep shows no description slot support.
otherGaps.suggestedFix: In DropZoneRoot, generate a stable description id, pass aria-describedby onto the Area's RAC DropZone (or the root domProps), and set that id on the Description span so the hint is programmatically linked.

otherGaps.title: Disabled state does not propagate to root/indicator (only the <select> dims)
otherGaps.detail: When Trigger receives disabled, only the native <select> gets the :disabled treatment. The root native-select div and the absolutely-positioned chevron Indicator (rendered as a sibling, not a descendant of <select>) get no disabled signal, so the indicator and any wrapper-level disabled styling do not respond. HeroUI typically dims the whole control including the indicator.
otherGaps.evidence: Trigger spreads disabled onto <select> via {...selectProps} (index.tsx:168-176) but never lifts it into context or onto the root div; root only sets aria-invalid/data-invalid (index.tsx:251-263), and Indicator renders outside the <select> (index.tsx:179).
otherGaps.suggestedFix: Read the Trigger's disabled prop into context (or have root accept it) and emit data-disabled on the root div and on the Indicator span so disabled styling/opacity covers the indicator and wrapper, not just the <select>.

otherGaps.title: Search query and active category reset on every popover close/reopen (no persistence across open)
otherGaps.detail: Search and active-category state live inside Panel, which is unmounted when the Popover closes. Each reopen starts fresh; there is no way to preserve or control the active category or query. Minor divergence from a stateful picker but not a documented contract break.
otherGaps.evidence: `const [activeCategory, setActiveCategory] = useState(...)` and `const [query, setQuery] = useState('')` (lines 132-133) are local to Panel, which is conditionally mounted only while the RACPopover (lines 309-324) is open; closing unmounts Panel and discards both.
otherGaps.suggestedFix: If persistence is desired, lift activeCategory/query to EmojiPicker or accept controlled props; otherwise document the reset-on-close behavior as intentional.

otherGaps.title: Off-screen slides are not hidden from the accessibility/tab order
otherGaps.detail: All slides remain in the DOM with no aria-hidden/inert on inactive slides (grep aria-hidden/inert returns nothing). Interactive content inside non-visible slides stays focusable and is read by assistive tech, diverging from typical carousel behavior where only the active slide is exposed.
otherGaps.evidence: index.tsx:189-198 Item has no aria-hidden/inert; onSelect (index.tsx:384-388) only tracks selectedIndex/canScroll flags and never toggles hidden state on items.
otherGaps.suggestedFix: On select/reInit, mark non-selected items with inert/aria-hidden (or rely on Embla's slidesInView) so background slides are removed from the tab order and AT.

otherGaps.title: No dedicated header-action slot; actions/legend share the same flex row
otherGaps.detail: The contract token 'header-actions' refers to HeroUI Pro's justify-between header where title sits left and actions/legend sit right. Widget.Header is a single flex row (.widget__header { justify-content: space-between }) with no Widget.Actions slot, so when both a Legend and action buttons are present they must be manually wrapped; the WidgetDemo instead places its week/month toggle Buttons in the Footer (data-display-demos.tsx:567-591) rather than the header.
otherGaps.evidence: index.tsx:37-45 Header is a plain <div data-slot="widget-header">; there is no Actions subcomponent in the Object.assign at index.tsx:124-132. Demo header at data-display-demos.tsx:490-499 contains only Title + Legend.
otherGaps.suggestedFix: Acceptable as-is since HeroUI Pro has no formal Actions slot, but consider documenting/adding a thin Widget.Actions wrapper (margin-inline-start:auto) so action buttons land on the header's right edge consistently next to or instead of the legend.

otherGaps.title: Icon nested inside ItemContent diverges from HeroUI's documented row composition
otherGaps.detail: HeroUI Pro documents the row as <Item><Icon/><ItemContent>title/preview...</ItemContent></Item> with Icon a sibling of ItemContent, but Vela's demos place ChatListView.Icon inside ChatListView.ItemContent (ai-overlay-demos.tsx:1130-1137). The component does not enforce or default any icon position, so consumers following the documented structure get a different slot tree. Purely structural; both arrangements render and the selection/compact behavior is unaffected.
otherGaps.evidence: src/showcase/demos/ai-overlay-demos.tsx:1130 renders <ChatListView.ItemContent><ChatListView.Icon/>...</ChatListView.ItemContent>, and index.tsx Icon (line 101) is just a div with class chat-list-view__icon, agnostic to placement.
otherGaps.suggestedFix: Optional: document/standardize Icon placement to match HeroUI (Icon as sibling of ItemContent) or have ItemContent slot the icon explicitly; no runtime behavior change required.

otherGaps.title: No per-message semantic distinction between user and assistant for assistive tech
otherGaps.detail: The user/assistant difference is conveyed only via the chat-message--user / chat-message--assistant CSS classes; the message root carries no role, aria-label, or visually-hidden speaker label, so a screen reader cannot tell who authored a given bubble (the role="log" live region lives only on the conversation container, not per message).
otherGaps.evidence: index.tsx Assistant (26-33) and User (36-44) emit plain <div> with only className/data-slot; the sole aria attribute in the file is aria-hidden on the avatar spacer (index.tsx:52). role="log" is in chat-conversation/index.tsx:184, not chat-message.
otherGaps.suggestedFix: Add an optional visually-hidden speaker label or aria-label on the assistant/user roots (e.g. "Assistant said" / "You said") so the role-layout distinction is also exposed to assistive tech, matching HeroUI's labeled bubbles.

otherGaps.title: Copy button has no tooltip; feedback is aria-label only
otherGaps.detail: HeroUI's copy button surfaces a hover affordance/label. Vela's CopyButton conveys state solely via the dynamic aria-label (getCopyLabel returns 'Copy code'/'Copied'/'Copy failed') and the icon; there is no visible Tooltip on hover/focus, so sighted users get no textual hint until after pressing.
otherGaps.evidence: index.tsx:348-368 Button is isIconOnly with only aria-label={getCopyLabel(...)}; no Tooltip wrapper or title attribute.
otherGaps.suggestedFix: Wrap the icon-only Button in a HeroUI Tooltip (content reflecting copy status) to match the hover affordance, or confirm via the reference that no tooltip is expected and leave as-is.

otherGaps.title: Hardcoded variant="secondary" not surfaced as a documented prop
otherGaps.detail: CheckboxGroupRoot hardcodes variant="secondary" before spreading ...rest. A caller can still override it (rest is spread after, line 153), but the typed CheckboxButtonGroupProps does not advertise variant as an intentional API and the default silently forces 'secondary'. This is a deliberate parity choice per the code comment, so it is low severity, but the variant axis HeroUI exposes is not type-discoverable.
otherGaps.evidence: src/components/checkbox-button-group/index.tsx:147 `variant="secondary"` placed before `{...rest}` (line 153); explanatory comment at lines 139-141.
otherGaps.suggestedFix: Keep 'secondary' as the default but surface variant as a documented prop (default 'secondary') so the supported variant range is explicit, and confirm the later-spread {...rest} override is intended.

otherGaps.title: Drop ignores the input's single-file (no multiple) constraint
otherGaps.detail: When the consumer omits 'multiple' on DropZone.Input (single-file intent), dropping several files still enqueues all of them: Area.handleDrop forwards every dropped file item to addDropItems → addFiles with no cap, and 'multiple' lives only on the hidden input (which drop bypasses). Accept/maxSize are validated on drop but file count is not.
otherGaps.evidence: Area.handleDrop (index.tsx lines 588-592) calls context?.addDropItems(event.items) unconditionally; addDropItems (lines 398-418) maps all isFileDropItem items; the 'multiple' attr is only read by the native input, never by the drop path.
otherGaps.suggestedFix: Track whether the input is multiple (e.g. via context flag set from DropZone.Input or a root prop) and in addDropItems/addFiles truncate or replace to a single file when multiple is false, mirroring native single-file picker behavior.

otherGaps.title: Second Description registration is silently ignored / order-fragile aria-describedby
otherGaps.detail: resolvedDescriptionId is hard-pinned to descriptionIds[0], so if more than one Description (or one that mounts after another) is present, only the first registered id is referenced by aria-describedby and the others are dropped. It also depends on mount order rather than DOM order. The single-Description contract works, but the registration list is effectively unused beyond [0].
otherGaps.evidence: resolvedDescriptionId = descriptionIds[0] ?? descriptionId (index.tsx:222); registerDescription appends ids into an array (index.tsx:213-221) but only index 0 is ever read.
otherGaps.suggestedFix: If multiple descriptions are intended, join all registered ids into aria-describedby (space-separated); otherwise simplify to a single registered id to make the limitation explicit.

otherGaps.title: Editing engine is deprecated document.execCommand rather than a real editor library
otherGaps.detail: The project's stated preference is real engines; this uses document.execCommand for all formatting, which is deprecated and inconsistent across browsers (e.g. strikeThrough, formatBlock nesting, list toggling edge cases). It is functional and produces real DOM mutations, so not a fake, but it is a divergence from the real-engine bar.
otherGaps.evidence: src/components/rich-text-editor/index.tsx:345-371 runCommand/setLink/unsetLink all call document.execCommand; commandToExec (188-211) maps every command to an execCommand string.
otherGaps.suggestedFix: Consider migrating the editing core to a real engine (TipTap/ProseMirror or Lexical) for robust command semantics, history, and selection handling; at minimum document the execCommand limitations.

otherGaps.title: No focus management when the mobile menu opens/closes
otherGaps.detail: Menu mounts/unmounts based on isMenuOpen (index.tsx:457-489) but never moves focus into the panel on open nor restores focus to the toggle on close. For a full-height disclosure panel this is the expected keyboard-overlay behavior; without it, keyboard focus stays on the toggle while a large panel covers the page.
otherGaps.evidence: Menu component (index.tsx:455-508) handles only height/opacity/presence; there is no useRef-based focus() call or focus restoration logic.
otherGaps.suggestedFix: On open, move focus to the menu container (or first MenuItem); on close, restore focus to the MenuToggle element.
