otherGaps.title: Auto-content path has no hideIndicator support
otherGaps.detail: HeroUI Pro lets the auto-content tooltip suppress the color indicator (hideIndicator) independently of label. The Vela auto-content renderer always passes an indicator and has no hideIndicator option.
otherGaps.evidence: src/components/line-chart/index.tsx:219-223 always sets `indicator={indicator}` on every ChartTooltip.Item; LineChartTooltipContentProps (lines 190-202) exposes hideLabel but no hideIndicator.
otherGaps.suggestedFix: Add a hideIndicator prop to the TooltipContent renderer and pass indicator={hideIndicator ? undefined : indicator}; the primitive already omits the indicator span when indicator is undefined (chart-tooltip/index.tsx:37).

otherGaps.title: Legend is re-exported raw with no series-toggle interaction
otherGaps.detail: LineChart.Legend (index.tsx:19,243) maps directly to Recharts' Legend with no wrapper. HeroUI Pro line-chart legends are typically clickable to toggle a series' visibility, but here clicking a legend item does nothing and no demo wires an onClick/hidden-state. This interaction is not part of the listed contract tokens, hence low severity.
otherGaps.evidence: index.tsx line 243: `Legend,` inside Object.assign — bare re-export, no onClick handler, no controlled visibility state, and no demo uses LineChart.Legend at all.
otherGaps.suggestedFix: If legend series-toggle parity is desired, wrap Legend with a stateful content renderer that toggles each Line's `hide` prop on click and reflects active state; otherwise document that legend toggling is out of scope.

otherGaps.title: Kpi root is a generic div with no labelling tie between Title and Value
otherGaps.detail: The KPI card is a plain div with data-slot='kpi' and no region/group role or aria-labelledby linking Title to Value, so assistive tech reads loose text fragments rather than a labelled metric. This is minor since axe passes, but it diverges from a self-describing metric widget.
otherGaps.evidence: index.tsx line 14-16: KpiRoot renders <div data-slot="kpi" className={clsx('kpi', className)} {...rest}/> with no role/aria; Title (line 40) and Value (line 45) are unrelated divs with no id/aria-labelledby wiring.
otherGaps.suggestedFix: Optionally generate an id on Title and set aria-labelledby on the root (or expose role/aria props) so the value is announced with its label; keep Icon aria-hidden as-is.

otherGaps.title: No loading/empty content-layout affordance
otherGaps.detail: The 'content-layout' token covers the elevated inner content area that hosts charts/tables/KPIs. The reference Widget itself documents no loading/empty/error state (confirmed via heroui.pro), and Vela also provides none — content is always the raw children. This is parity-correct, noting only that any skeleton/empty handling is left entirely to the consumer.
otherGaps.evidence: index.tsx:67-75 Content is a passthrough <div data-slot="widget-content"> with no isLoading/empty handling; CSS .widget__content (widget.css:39-56) only styles the surface/shadow.
otherGaps.suggestedFix: No change required for parity; if desired, a future isLoading prop could render a skeleton inside Content, but HeroUI Pro does not document one so this is optional polish.

otherGaps.title: Streaming reasoning updates are not announced (no aria-live/aria-busy)
otherGaps.detail: During streaming the trigger/content text changes are not exposed to assistive tech; there is no aria-busy on the root or aria-live region, so screen-reader users get no signal that reasoning is in progress or updating.
otherGaps.evidence: grep for aria-live|aria-busy|role= in index.tsx returns nothing; isStreaming only toggles the shimmer span and the root modifier class (lines 71-77, 137-148).
otherGaps.suggestedFix: When isStreaming, set aria-busy="true" on the root (or wrap streaming text in an aria-live="polite" region) so updates are conveyed; keep aria-hidden off the meaningful text.

otherGaps.title: scrollToBottom is not exposed via imperative ref; ref forwards the raw viewport element only
otherGaps.detail: useImperativeHandle returns viewportRef.current (the raw HTMLDivElement), so consumers who want to programmatically jump to the latest message after an external content update must reach into context (unavailable outside the tree) or reimplement scrollHeight math. Composed chat UIs commonly need an imperative scrollToBottom handle.
otherGaps.evidence: src/components/chat-conversation/index.tsx line 88: useImperativeHandle(forwardedRef, () => viewportRef.current as HTMLDivElement, []). scrollToBottom (line 99) is only passed through React context, not on the ref.
otherGaps.suggestedFix: Expose an imperative handle that augments the element with { scrollToBottom, isAtBottom } (or a dedicated methods object), so consumers can trigger stick-to-bottom on demand without the context.

otherGaps.title: No disclosure indicator (chevron) in the expandable trigger
otherGaps.detail: An expandable disclosure header conventionally renders a rotating chevron/caret to signal expand state. Vela's trigger renders only the label and the status dot; there is no caret element, so the only expand affordance is the status indicator (which also doubles as run-state). Minor, but diverges from the reference's collapsible header affordance.
otherGaps.evidence: src/components/chat-tool/index.tsx:82-90 trigger contains only `.chat-tool__trigger-label` and `.chat-tool__status`; grep for chevron/caret/svg in index.tsx and chat-tool.css returns no disclosure indicator (only the `.chat-tool__status-indicator` dot).
otherGaps.suggestedFix: Render a chevron element inside the expandable trigger that rotates based on `aria-expanded`/`data-expanded`, conditioned on `isExpandable`.

otherGaps.title: Selected/pressed state is not modeled by the component
otherGaps.detail: Demos express selection via raw aria-pressed on each Item (ai-overlay-demos.tsx:650, 657, 669, 677), but the component itself has no selected/isSelected prop and applies no selected class/data attribute, so visual + ARIA selected styling is entirely the consumer's responsibility and is inconsistently applied (the variant demo at 2583/2592 sets no aria-pressed at all).
otherGaps.evidence: src/components/prompt-suggestion/index.tsx:61-73 Item has no `selected`/`isSelected` prop and emits no data-selected/aria-pressed of its own.
otherGaps.suggestedFix: Add an optional selected prop on Item/CardItem that sets aria-pressed and a data-selected attribute, so selection state is a real part of the component API rather than ad-hoc consumer markup.

otherGaps.title: No guard for non-finite values
otherGaps.detail: Passing NaN or a non-finite value formats to literal 'NaN'/'∞' with no fallback, which is a rough edge for a feedback/display component fed by live metrics.
otherGaps.evidence: index.tsx:28 calls `.format(value)` unconditionally with no isFinite check or placeholder.
otherGaps.suggestedFix: Add an optional fallback (e.g. render a dash/placeholder) when `Number.isFinite(value)` is false.

otherGaps.title: Validation-failed files cannot be retried, only uploaded files can
otherGaps.detail: Files that fail accept/maxSize validation are created with canRetry:false, so the retry trigger never renders for them; only simulated-upload failures (shouldFailUpload) set canRetry:true. HeroUI documents a generic retry trigger for the failed state; here a validation failure is a terminal state with no recover-in-place affordance (user must remove and re-add).
otherGaps.evidence: createQueueItem sets canRetry:false for validation errors (index.tsx line 372); DefaultFileQueueItem only renders FileRetryTrigger when item.status==='failed' && item.canRetry (lines 1000-1002); only startUploadTimer sets canRetry:true on simulated failure (lines 477-486).
otherGaps.suggestedFix: This is likely intentional (re-validating the same file would fail identically), but if parity requires a retry affordance on all failed items, allow retryFile to re-run validation for validation-failed items too and set canRetry accordingly.

otherGaps.title: Item itemKey path drops user-supplied isDisabled for navigation but not vice versa; scroll fires even when target id is missing
otherGaps.detail: scrollToItem always calls setActive(key) and onActiveChange before checking the target exists (index.tsx:481-483), so an item whose targetId/key has no DOM element still reports an active change with no scroll — divergent from 'navigate to section' semantics where a missing anchor should be a no-op.
otherGaps.evidence: setActive(key) runs at index.tsx:481, the target null-check returns only afterward at line 483 ('if (target === null) return;').
otherGaps.suggestedFix: Resolve and verify the target element before committing the active change, or document that active follows intent regardless of target presence; at minimum guard so a missing target does not desync active highlight from scroll position.

otherGaps.title: Ranked/list content has no list/listitem ARIA structure
otherGaps.detail: The container is only role="group" and child ItemCards are role="button" (when interactive) or have no role. For ranked-content (e.g. 'Top courses by enrollment' with rank badges) there is no role="list"/listitem or ordered-list semantics, so assistive tech gets no count/position context for the ranked items.
otherGaps.evidence: index.tsx:119 `role="group"` only; item-card/index.tsx:204 `resolvedRole = role ?? (isInteractionEnabled ? 'button' : undefined)` — no listitem; demo ranks are visual Badge "1"/"2" only (data-display-demos.tsx:206,218).
otherGaps.suggestedFix: Allow the group to opt into list semantics (e.g. role="list" on the container and role="listitem" on items, or a rank/order prop), so ranked lists expose position/size to assistive tech instead of relying on visual badges.

otherGaps.title: No upload progress / loading or error state on the preview tile
otherGaps.detail: A composer-oriented attachment tile in HeroUI Pro surfaces upload state (loading/progress, error). Vela's tile renders only a static preview + name + remove button (index.tsx:16-42) with no progress/loading/error affordance or prop, so an in-flight upload cannot be represented without faking it at the demo level.
otherGaps.evidence: ChatAttachmentProps has no status/progress/isLoading/error fields (index.tsx:6-12); render contains no spinner/progress element.
otherGaps.suggestedFix: Add an optional status/progress prop and render a real progress indicator or error badge over the preview, mirroring HeroUI Pro's uploading state.

otherGaps.title: Ripple's minimumPressDuration/duration are mutable per render but pointer listeners are bound once
otherGaps.detail: Shaping props are read live via shapingRef (good), but the whole feedback design only re-binds listeners when isDisabled changes (effect dep [isDisabled]). This is acceptable for shaping, but combined with the keyboard gap means there is no path at all (controlled or otherwise) to invoke ripple/press feedback programmatically — only raw pointer gestures.
otherGaps.evidence: index.tsx:171 effect dep array is [isDisabled]; shapingRef.current is refreshed at 90-91 but interaction entry points remain pointer-only.
otherGaps.suggestedFix: If parity needs programmatic/controlled press feedback, expose an imperative trigger or honor the host's pressed data-attribute so press-state can be driven without a pointer; otherwise document the pointer-only limitation.

otherGaps.title: Custom Indicator children dropped when null/false rather than allowing icon-less trigger
otherGaps.detail: Indicator falls back to the default chevron with `children ?? <ChevronsExpandVerticalIcon/>`, which is correct for undefined, but HeroUI Pro variants that hide the indicator entirely (passing null) would still get the default icon injected, diverging from a no-indicator cell layout.
otherGaps.evidence: index.tsx:142 `{children ?? <ChevronsExpandVerticalIcon />}` — only `undefined`/`null` both coalesce, so an explicit `null` child still yields the default chevron.
otherGaps.suggestedFix: If a no-indicator variant is in the reference, distinguish explicit `null` (render nothing) from absent children (render default chevron).

otherGaps.title: Invalid state sets aria-invalid but provides no programmatic error message association
otherGaps.detail: The invalid-state demo renders a Description as the error helper, but the component does not mark it as an error message (no role=alert / aria-errormessage), and the description is associated only via generic aria-describedby. Assistive tech announces it as a description, not an error, and there is no required/aria-required wiring tied to validity.
otherGaps.evidence:
Trigger only sets aria-invalid (index.tsx:157,174); Description always renders a plain

with data-slot native-select-description and no error role/aria-errormessage (index.tsx:126-133).
otherGaps.suggestedFix: When the control is invalid, expose the helper text via aria-errormessage (or role=alert) instead of/in addition to aria-describedby so screen readers announce it as an error.

otherGaps.title: MenuToggle is open-only; cannot close the mobile sidebar
otherGaps.detail: MenuToggle hardcodes opening the sidebar and provides no way to close it, so without a Sheet backdrop or a close affordance the mobile sidebar cannot be dismissed via the toggle, diverging from HeroUI Pro's toggle behavior.
otherGaps.evidence: `const handlePress = useCallback(() => { ctx?.setSidebarOpen(true); }, [ctx]);` — always `true`, never toggles, and aria-label is fixed to 'Open navigation'.
otherGaps.suggestedFix: Have MenuToggle call `ctx?.toggleSidebar()` (or pair it with a Sheet backdrop onOpenChange) and reflect state via aria-expanded/data-state so the mobile sidebar can be closed.

otherGaps.title: Disabled state only disables the trigger button, not the picker value/overlay
otherGaps.detail: Disabled is applied via `<CellColorPicker.Trigger isDisabled>` on the RAC Button only; the root `CellColorPicker` (RAC ColorPicker) has no isDisabled wiring, so the controlled value and any programmatically-opened overlay/area/slider remain interactive. HeroUI's disabled cell variant gates the whole picker.
otherGaps.evidence: Root `CellColorPickerProps = Omit<ColorPickerProps,'className'|'variant'>` with no isDisabled handling; demo uses `<CellColorPicker.Trigger isDisabled>` while the surrounding `<CellColorPicker defaultValue="#EF4444">` is unguarded.
otherGaps.suggestedFix: Accept `isDisabled` on `CellColorPickerRoot` and propagate it to the Trigger via context (and disable Area/Slider/SwatchPicker children) so the entire cell is inert, matching HeroUI's disabled variant.

otherGaps.title: No checkbox (selectionMode="multiple") items variant — only single-select radio is wired in demo
otherGaps.detail: The contract token is "checkbox-radio-items" (both modes), and HeroUI Pro shows both a checkbox (multiple) and radio (single) selection section, but only single-selection is exercised: ContextMenu.Menu selectionMode="single" defaultSelectedKeys={['preview']}. The component passes selectionMode through to HeroMenu so the multiple/checkbox capability exists but is never demonstrated, leaving checkbox-item parity unverified.
otherGaps.evidence: with-selection variant uses only `selectionMode="single"` with `<ContextMenu.ItemIndicator />`; no `selectionMode="multiple"` usage anywhere in feedback-nav-demos.tsx.
otherGaps.suggestedFix: Add a checkbox variant exercising `selectionMode="multiple"` with ItemIndicator to confirm multi-select indicator/toggle parity; no index.tsx change needed since selectionMode already forwards to HeroMenu.

otherGaps.title: Collapsed icon-rail nested menus have no flyout/popover for children
otherGaps.detail: With collapsible="icon", RAC Tree only expands children inline; when the rail is collapsed to icons there is no popover/flyout to reveal a parent's nested items, so nested-navigation is unreachable in collapsed state. HeroUI Pro surfaces children as a hover/click flyout when collapsed.
otherGaps.evidence: Submenu is a pass-through `(<>{children}</>)` and Menu is a plain RAC `<Tree>` with no collapsed-state popover branch; expansion is inline-only (lines 363-374, 559).
otherGaps.suggestedFix: In collapsed icon state, render parent items' children inside a Popover/flyout (e.g. HeroUI Popover or RAC) triggered on hover/click of the icon row instead of relying on inline Tree expansion.

otherGaps.title: No collapse trigger / rail to toggle the desktop sidebar
otherGaps.detail: The icon-collapsible sidebar width is driven entirely by CSS variables from open state, but the component exposes no built-in collapse trigger or rail; consumers must wire their own button (as the demo does), whereas HeroUI Pro ships a sidebar trigger/rail control as part of the layout.
otherGaps.evidence: `sidebarCollapsible='icon'` only affects `sidebarWidthVar` (collapsedSidebarWidth); the only exported triggers are `MenuToggle` (mobile, open-only) and `AsideTrigger`. Demo hand-rolls `<Button onClick={handleToggle}>收起侧栏</Button>`.
otherGaps.suggestedFix: Expose an `AppLayout.SidebarTrigger`/Rail subcomponent that calls `toggleSidebar()` and reflects collapsed/expanded data-state, matching HeroUI Pro's collapse control.

otherGaps.title: Sidebar resizable silently disabled in icon-collapsible (default) mode
otherGaps.detail: `sidebarResizable` only takes effect when collapsible is 'offcanvas' or 'none', so with the default `collapsible='icon'` a consumer setting `sidebarResizable` gets no resize handle and no warning — a silent no-op that diverges from HeroUI Pro's resizable sidebar areas.
otherGaps.evidence: `const resolvedSidebarResizable = hasSidebar && sidebarResizable && (sidebarCollapsible === 'offcanvas' || sidebarCollapsible === 'none');`
otherGaps.suggestedFix: Either support resizing in icon mode (resize between collapsed/expanded bounds) or document/warn; at minimum allow resize to coexist with icon collapse as HeroUI Pro does.

otherGaps.title: persisted-state restores nothing on mount (write-only cookie)
otherGaps.detail: The component writes `sidebar_state`/`aside_state` cookies but never reads them to initialize open state, so persisted collapse state is not restored on the client after navigation/reload, unlike HeroUI Pro which hydrates from the persisted cookie.
otherGaps.evidence: Two effects only `document.cookie = ...`; `useControllableOpen` initializes from `defaultSidebarOpen`/`defaultAsideOpen` with no cookie read.
otherGaps.suggestedFix: On mount (uncontrolled), read the `sidebar_state`/`aside_state` cookie and seed initial open state so persistence round-trips.

otherGaps.title: Submenu Popover has no accessible name while root Popover does
otherGaps.detail: ContextMenuPopover sets aria-label="Context menu" on its RAC Popover (role="dialog"), but ContextMenuSubmenuPopover renders a bare RAC <Popover> with no aria-label/aria-labelledby, so the submenu overlay's dialog node lacks an accessible name — inconsistent with the root and the same class of axe dialog-name issue the test file flags.
otherGaps.evidence: Root: `<Popover aria-label="Context menu" ...>` (line 385); Submenu: `ContextMenuSubmenuPopover` renders `<Popover offset placement className style {...rest}>` with no aria-label (lines 454-462).
otherGaps.suggestedFix: Default an aria-label (or label association with the inner submenu Menu) on the SubmenuPopover's RAC Popover, mirroring the root ContextMenuPopover.

otherGaps.title: Rail only toggles collapse — no drag-to-resize width
otherGaps.detail: The Rail is a click-to-toggle button (tabIndex={-1}, onClick→toggleSidebar) with a resize cursor but no pointer-drag handler, so the sidebar width is fixed. HeroUI Pro's rail supports drag-to-resize; the resize cursor here implies an affordance the component does not deliver.
otherGaps.evidence: `<button ... tabIndex={-1} aria-label="Toggle sidebar" onClick={handleClick} />` where handleClick only calls toggleSidebar() — no onPointerDown/move resize logic (lines 578-598).
otherGaps.suggestedFix: Add optional pointer-drag resizing on Rail (onPointerDown/move writing a width CSS var) gated behind a prop, or drop the resize cursor to avoid implying resize support.

otherGaps.title: No hex/text color field for direct value entry
otherGaps.detail: The component exposes Area, Slider, SwatchPicker, Swatch, Label and ValueDisplay but no editable hex/channel input subcomponent; HeroUI's color picker variants typically include a text field for typing a color value, and the contract implies 6 variant sections.
otherGaps.evidence: Exported subcomponents in index.tsx are Trigger, Label, ValueDisplay, Swatch, Popover, Area, Slider, SwatchPicker only — ValueDisplay is a read-only `<span>` (`state.color.toString('hex')`), never an input.
otherGaps.suggestedFix: Add a `CellColorPicker.Field`/`ColorField` (or editable hex input) subcomponent wrapping RAC ColorField so users can type a value, covering the format/input variant.

perComponentSummaries.summary: Real recharts 3.x wrapper (ResponsiveContainer + AreaChart) with genuine gradient fills, hover/keyboard tooltips, and multi/stacked series across all 6 documented demos; contract tokens are all really implemented, but the public API is thinner than its sibling bar-chart and omits chart-level event handlers and a Legend subcomponent.

Real recharts 3.x wrapper (ResponsiveContainer + BarChart + Bar/XAxis/YAxis/Tooltip/Legend, plus a custom ChartTooltip-based TooltipContent renderer); all four contract tokens are genuinely implemented with the real charting engine, and all 8 documented variants are wired to real components in the demo.

The standalone chart-tooltip is a real, self-implemented presentational primitive (Root/Header/Item with role="tooltip", dot/line indicators, payload-color indicators) — no fakes/iframes — and the auto-content + formatter behavior is genuinely wired in LineChart.TooltipContent which composes it; but the documented ChartTooltip itself exposes none of the formatter/auto-content API and is missing the dashed indicator variant.

Real Recharts 3.8.1 implementation: ResponsiveContainer + ComposedChart with Bar/Line/Area re-exported as subcomponents and a genuine custom Tooltip content renderer wired through Recharts' content callback — not a fake. The three contract tokens (responsive-chart, tooltip-hover, mixed-series) are functionally met; the gaps are in chart-level prop forwarding/accessibility, not in the rendering engine.

Real, well-built Recharts 3.8 wrapper: ResponsiveContainer for responsive sizing, real Recharts Tooltip driving a custom role="tooltip" content component, multiple Line children for multi-series, and a dedicated axes-free sparkline variant — no fakes; gaps are thin test coverage and a raw non-interactive Legend re-export.

Real recharts 3.8 wrapper (ResponsiveContainer + Pie/Cell/Label/Tooltip/Legend re-exports) with a genuine custom TooltipContent on the chart-tooltip BEM component; responsive-chart and tooltip-hover are solidly implemented, but active-slice hover-expand is absent and the legend token is only shown via an external Widget.Legend, never recharts Legend.

Real, self-implemented wrapper over recharts 3.8.1 (ResponsiveContainer + PolarGrid/PolarAngleAxis/PolarRadiusAxis/Radar + real Tooltip rendering through the in-repo ChartTooltip DOM); all three contract tokens (responsive-chart, polar-grid, tooltip-hover) are genuinely wired and all 5 reference variants are exercised in the demo, with a few real but minor gaps around test coverage, tooltip cursor, and accessibility.

Real recharts 3.8.1 wrapper: ResponsiveContainer for responsive-chart, a real recharts Tooltip wired to a composed ChartTooltip BEM renderer for tooltip-hover, and RadialBar/Cell for progress-arc — all three contract tokens are backed by real code with full pointer/touch/mouse handler pass-through; gaps are a demo-level arc-scaling bug, absent keyboard/ARIA a11y, and thin tests.

A genuinely real, self-implemented compound component (useAgenda hook + ~20 composed subcomponents) with real pointer-based drag/resize/create, 15-min snapping, controllable view/date/selection APIs, a live current-time indicator, and greedy all-day/month spanning layout — not a fake; but several documented contract behaviors are keyboard-only or missing (pointer delete, month-overflow popover, drag in month/all-day) and the calendar grid lacks ARIA grid semantics.

A genuine, real implementation: thin wrapper over HeroUI's RAC-based Toolbar (role="toolbar", roving-tabindex keyboard nav, data-slot) with a controlled isOpen API, enter/exit animation + deferred unmount, and composed Prefix/Content/Suffix/Label slots; demos drive it from real DataGrid/ListView selection with a real dismiss button — but the animationend-based unmount path is dead code, so exit timing is hardcoded rather than tied to the actual animation.

Real, well-built wrapper over Embla (useEmblaCarousel) with composed subcomponents, real prev/next/dots/thumbnails handlers, keyboard nav, and a genuine Embla API surfaced via setApi/useCarousel; the main parity gaps are an autoplay helper that doesn't pause on hover/focus/interaction and missing per-slide live-region/labeling accessibility.

A genuine real-engine wrapper over HeroUI OSS Table (RAC-backed): sorting, selection, and column-resize are wired to real react-aria-components primitives (allowsSorting/sortDescriptor, slot="selection" checkboxes, ResizableTableContainer/ColumnResizer with the correct onResize(Map) signature), plus real scroll-driven virtualization and Enter/blur/Escape cell editing — no fakes, but a few behavioral divergences exist around column width and the always-mounted inline editor.

A real, self-implemented compound component (plain forwardRef div slots + clsx, no fakes); demos pass real HeroUI Buttons with working onClick state, so the action-buttons and visual-state (sm/md/lg) contracts are structurally met. The gaps are DOM/ARIA semantics: Title/Description render as generic divs (reference uses h3/p), the root carries an unwarranted role="status" live region, and no polymorphic `as` prop exists to let consumers correct it.

A genuine, real-engine implementation: built on react-aria-components Tree (real expansion via RAC toggleKey, real single/multi selection with HeroUI Checkbox wired through slot="selection", real keyboard navigation, real drag-drop via useDragAndDrop + useTreeData) with a custom CollectionRoot that injects Motion height/opacity transitions for expand/collapse; the only real parity gap is a hardcoded chevron aria-label that clobbers RAC's localized state-aware label.

A genuinely real implementation: RAC Popover anchored to the trigger ref for the sticky panel, a real rAF-throttled scroll-spy effect (scroll/resize listeners + getBoundingClientRect threshold) wired to active state, and real smooth-scroll anchor navigation for both window and container; main gaps are missing TOC accessibility semantics (aria-current, navigation/list landmark, trigger popup wiring) rather than fakery.

Real, self-implemented wrapper: a custom HoverCardRoot manages controlled/uncontrolled open state plus open/close delay timers, and the overlay is a genuine react-aria-components Popover (positioning, portal, entering/exiting data attrs, OverlayArrow) wired via triggerRef+isOpen; hover (pointer), keyboard (Enter/Space/Escape) and focus-visible triggers are all real. The main gap is missing ARIA association between trigger and content.

A genuine real-engine implementation (Motion Reorder.Group/Item + useDragControls, FLIP animations, real pointer-driven cross-column hit detection, and a useState data layer) — not a fake — but it diverges from HeroUI Pro's react-aria DnD contract on keyboard accessibility, drop-position feedback, and list semantics.

Real, self-implemented pressable card (plain DOM + native React pointer/keyboard/click/focus handlers, no fakes): it wires genuine press-action (onPress on click + Enter/Space with data-pressed/data-focus-visible), controlled+uncontrolled selection with proper aria-pressed, inner-interactive press suppression, and composed Icon/Content/Title/Description/Action metadata slots plus five layout variants — fully covering the press-action, metadata-slots, and layout-variants contract.

Real self-implemented wrapper: plain composed markup (Root + Header/Title/Description slots) that enhances ItemCard children via cloneElement to wire genuine press/selection handlers; the underlying ItemCard has real pointer/keyboard/focus-visible interaction with an interactive-child press guard. All 12 HeroUI variant sections exist in the demo and item-actions are solid, but the group selection API is single-select only (can't model the multi-toggle variants), the documented list "auto-dividers between cards" are never injected, and there are no list/listitem ARIA semantics.

Kpi is a pure layout primitive — 11 forwardRef <div> wrappers with BEM classes and one static data-status on Icon; it implements none of HeroUI Pro's documented behavioral APIs (value-formatting, trend-state, progress value), externalizing every behavior to sibling components and the caller, and Kpi.Progress emits no progressbar semantics.

A thin but mostly-faithful layout wrapper: RAC Group root with an orientation className and a decorative aria-hidden separator span — matching HeroUI's anatomy and decorative-separator semantics — but the documented responsive-layout behavior is entirely unimplemented.

Real react-aria-components GridList wrapper: keyboard nav, typeahead, single/multiple/none selection, disabledKeys, auto-rendered selection Checkbox, controlled/uncontrolled selectedKeys, and optional RAC Virtualizer are all genuinely wired — selection and action-bar contract tokens are solid. The main gap is the empty-state primitive (ListView.EmptyState is a disconnected styled div, not wired to GridList's renderEmptyState and never demoed).

Timeline is a clean, self-implemented compound component built from semantic markup (ol/li/h3/p/time) plus data-attributes and CSS — appropriate since HeroUI Pro's Timeline is presentational, not engine-driven; all four contract tokens (axis-placement, status-markers, action-buttons, current-item) have real mechanisms and the demo wires real Button onClick handlers, but the current-item state is visual-only (no aria-current) and the component ships with zero test coverage.

Widget is a real, faithfully-composed structural primitive — all 7 HeroUI Pro slots (Root/Header/Title/Description/Content/Footer/Legend/LegendItem) are implemented with forwarded refs, polymorphic root via @heroui/react dom.div, and a genuinely interactive LegendItem that renders a real focusable button when isPressable; no fakes. Minor gaps are around header-action affordances and pressable-item semantics rather than missing behavior.

A thin but genuine wrapper over the real RAC-based @heroui/react Disclosure (real keyboard/aria/expand-collapse, controlled + uncontrolled) with a real CSS text-shimmer animation for streaming; collapsible-steps and streaming-state are solid, but the status-icons contract has no implementation (steps are a single static dot with no status/icon states) and the documented nested "Agent Trace" variants are absent.

ChatAttachment is a plain static-markup tile (forwardRef div + img/video/fallback + a real remove button) with no composed subcomponents; it implements attachment-preview and remove-action at a basic level but is missing the grouping and composer/file-input pieces the HeroUI Pro contract documents, so two of the three documented variants are faked at the demo layer.

A genuinely real implementation: MutationObserver-driven stick-to-bottom (childList + characterData + subtree for streaming), real motion/react message entrance animation, real OSS Button + Tooltip scroll-to-bottom control, and controlled props (followOutput, threshold, initialScrollToBottom, onAtBottomChange); the only real parity gaps are missing resize handling and a couple of API/semantics divergences from HeroUI.

Real react-aria-components GridList/GridListItem wrapper with genuine keyboard navigation, single-select highlight, full controlled/uncontrolled selection API passthrough, and a real compact density backed by runtime-loaded CSS; both contract tokens (conversation-selection, compact-layout) are implemented with real behavior.

Real, self-implemented component: a compound ChatLoader with Dots/Pulse/Spinner/Skeleton variants built from plain semantic markup plus the in-repo Spinner SVG; dots/pulse/spinner animate via CSS that genuinely loads at runtime (lib.css -> chat-loader.css), but the documented skeleton "loading-animation" is missing — skeleton placeholders render fully static.

A thin but real, self-implemented compound primitive: plain forwardRef divs with data-slot/BEM classes for role-layout, a real Avatar in the avatar-slot, and markdown-content delegated to the real Markdown parser plus real Button/ToggleButton copy actions via chat-message-actions; no fakes, iframes, or static screenshots, and all three contract tokens are genuinely present.

Mostly real: Copy uses navigator.clipboard with an execCommand fallback and a copied/failed feedback cycle, ThumbsUp/ThumbsDown are real RAC ToggleButtons (controlled isSelected + onChange + aria-pressed), and the demo wires the menu trigger into a real Dropdown/Menu/Popover — no fakes. The notable gaps are a faked-out (static) copy→check icon animation despite motion being an available dependency, and the Action wrapper silently dropping Button override props.

chat-source is plain static markup: a bare anchor wrapped in dead hover-card class names, with no real popover/hover-card engine, no confidence state, and no document/grouped/stacked-favicon semantics — the library's real RAC-based HoverCard exists but is never used here.

A genuinely real, self-implemented disclosure component: native <button> trigger with aria-expanded/aria-controls, controlled+uncontrolled expand API, a 7-state status machine with aria-busy, and composed Args/Result/Error/Approval/Meta subcomponents — no fakes. Gaps are against HeroUI Pro's documented variants: a missing ChatToolGroup, no real expand/collapse animation, and no custom-trigger composable slot.

A real self-implemented wrapper: Root/Header/Code/CopyButton subcomponents over OSS Button, with a genuine navigator.clipboard copy + execCommand fallback, 2s icon-swap feedback, and a hand-rolled regex tokenizer for highlighting; the main parity gaps are non-Shiki highlighting limited to 4 languages, a hardcoded/static icon "motion" placeholder instead of a real transition, and a non-keyboard-scrollable code region.

The Markdown component is a hand-rolled regex parser (not react-markdown/remark-gfm/streamdown as documented) that renders headings, inline marks, links, lists, tables and CodeBlock-backed fences correctly for the default case, but it omits the streaming-memoization optimization, the entire Streamdown variant (incomplete-markdown repair, streaming animation, active caret), link security attributes, images, and nested/GFM list semantics.

A genuinely composed, real-engine implementation (HeroUI OSS Button/TextArea/ScrollShadow/Tooltip + Motion Reorder for queue drag, real autosize via ResizeObserver, controlled+uncontrolled value, Enter/Shift+Enter, IME-safe submit) — but the "attachments" contract is presentational only (no drag-and-drop drop zone) and queue reorder has no keyboard path.

Built as plain composable markup (no engine needed, and none required for this AI empty-state component): real native <button> Items/CardItems that pass onClick through via {...rest}, real header/group/title/description slots, and both documented variants (pill list + card layout). The core selection-action and card-layout contracts are genuinely implemented; gaps are confined to selection-API ergonomics and list/a11y semantics.

Real, faithful wrapper: a forwardRef span with data-slot="text-shimmer" + className passthrough, matching HeroUI Pro's children+className-only API, currentColor-driven color, and .text-shimmer root; the shimmer "engine" lives entirely in CSS which IS loaded at runtime (index.css -> lib.css -> components/text-shimmer.css). Not a fake. The one real gap is a broken CSS custom property that can void the gradient.

Plain markup wrapper around `new Intl.NumberFormat(locale, formatOptions).format(value)` with prefix/value/suffix slots; number-formatting is solid, but the documented animated-change behavior is entirely absent and locale-options ignores HeroUI's I18nProvider/useLocale integration.

Real implementation: RAC Button root plus four composed feedback layers (Highlight via CSS :hover/:active + data-hovered/data-pressed, Ripple via WAAPI ::after grow animation, HoldConfirm/ProgressFeedback via clip-path sweeps driven by real pointer/click handlers and stateful timers); CSS is genuinely loaded at runtime through lib.css. The one real contract gap is keyboard-press: the Ripple (and pointer-only HoldConfirm) ignore keyboard activation while the Highlight honors it.

Rating is a real RAC RadioGroup/Radio wrapper with working click + arrow-key selection, isReadOnly/isDisabled passthrough, controlled/uncontrolled numeric API, and read-only fractional half-star overlay; however the documented hover-preview behavior is entirely absent and half-step exists only as read-only display (no interactive half-star selection).

Thin static wrapper around the in-house Chip: it maps trend->color and renders a hardcoded arrow, value, and optional suffix, but exposes no custom-indicator, no prefix, and no number-formatting API, so 3 of the 6 documented sections are faked in the demo rather than supported by the component, and the only runtime CSS (heroui-full.css) has zero trend-chip rules.

Real, engine-backed implementation: a thin wrapper over react-resizable-panels v4.11.2 (Group/Panel/Separator), so panel-resize, keyboard-resize (Arrows/Home/End/Enter/F6 + full role="separator"/aria-valuenow ARIA), nested-groups, persisted-layout (localStorage bridge), css-variables, and imperative APIs are all genuinely functional; gaps are narrowed/typed-API divergences from the HeroUI Pro contract, not fakes.

cell-select is a genuine, thin compound wrapper over @heroui/react's real RAC-backed Select (popover, keyboard nav, controlled/uncontrolled value, disabled all delegated to the real engine) — no fakes; the only real parity gap is that the documented "custom-value" behavior is never actually implemented in the demo, plus a couple of minor API/coverage divergences.

Real implementation: a thin composed wrapper over HeroUI's RAC-backed Slider (Slider.Track/Fill/Thumb/Output), so slider-drag, controlled/uncontrolled value, step, and disabled all come from the genuine react-aria engine; the only real divergence is that CellSlider.Label is a detached <span> not wired to the slider's accessible name.

cell-switch is a real, self-implemented wrapper over the genuine HeroUI/react-aria-components Switch engine (SwitchField/SwitchButton/SwitchControl/SwitchThumb), with the full row (the RAC <label>) as the clickable trigger, a hidden native input[role=switch], real controlled+uncontrolled APIs, real keyboard/pointer interaction, and correct disabled handling — fully satisfying its toggle-state, controlled-value, and disabled-state contract.

Real implementation: a thin self-wrapper over HeroUI's @heroui/react CheckboxGroup/Checkbox (which is itself react-aria-components 1.17.0 CheckboxGroup), composing Item/Indicator/ItemContent/ItemIcon subcomponents with real keyboard/pointer interaction, render-prop children, and optional ripple feedback; multi-select, controlled-value, and disabled-state are genuinely implemented and tested, but the documented validation-state token has no first-class surface or coverage.

Real, fully-composed implementation: wraps react-aria-components DropZone (genuine useDrop drag highlight via data-drop-target, keyboard focus, clipboard paste-to-drop) plus RAC Button triggers, a real hidden file input, a real setInterval-driven upload state machine with controlled+uncontrolled queue, accept/maxSize/custom validation, retry/remove, and the OSS @heroui/react ProgressBar; tests cover all five contract tokens. No fakes, iframes, or placeholders.

inline-select is a real, thin composition wrapper over HeroUI OSS Select (which is built on react-aria-components Select/ListBox), so popover listbox, keyboard nav/typeahead, controlled+uncontrolled value, multi-select, disabled, and indicator data-open all come from a genuine engine — it fully implements the documented contract (inline-trigger, popover-select, controlled-value) and all 5 reference variants are demoed with real components.

Real, self-implemented compound wrapper around the browser-native <select> (correct architecture for a "native select"): React context wires id/label/aria-describedby/validation, controlled+uncontrolled value passes through, options/optgroups/disabled-options/disabled-select all use real native elements, and a default-or-custom Indicator is composed in; all 12 documented HeroUI variants are exercised by real demos — only a few minor DOM/state-propagation gaps exist.

Genuine thin wrapper over HeroUI RadioGroup/Radio (which wrap react-aria-components), forwarding all props via ...rest; single-select, controlled+uncontrolled value, disabled, and validation props are all wired through the real RAC engine, all 11 HeroUI Pro variants have real composed demos, and the test suite covers the documented contract.

A real, self-implemented contentEditable wrapper using document.execCommand as its editing engine with working toolbar toggle state, controlled/uncontrolled value, link popover, and live character count; but it has no test coverage, does not enforce maxLength, and its BubbleMenu/FloatingMenu/SuggestionMenu are inert presentational containers with no selection-anchored positioning or trigger logic.

A genuinely real implementation: semantic <nav> + RAC ToggleButton for the menu toggle, real controlled/uncontrolled menu state, a real scroll listener for hideOnScroll, a real two-phase height/opacity menu transition, real navigate-based client routing with external/forceReload branches, and the demo wires a real Dropdown for dropdown-actions; gaps are in disclosure ARIA wiring, reduced-motion honoring, and Escape/focus keyboard behavior.

Real react-aria-components implementation (DialogTrigger/Popover/Dialog + ListBox grid + ToggleButtonGroup + Input) with genuine overlay, focus management, keyboard nav and controlled/uncontrolled value — popover-grid and selection-action are solid, but the skin-tone contract is entirely absent and search-filter is non-functional for real emoji lookup.

Real implementation: @heroui/react Drawer (RAC DialogTrigger/ModalOverlay/Modal/Dialog) as the modal/focus-trap base, with a Motion (framer-motion) drag layer on Sheet.Dialog for drag-to-dismiss and discrete snap stepping; all 16 HeroUI variant sections have real composed demos. Core gaps are in snap-point fidelity (no continuous follow / nearest-snap), a missing isModal prop, and a missing preventCycle prop documented by HeroUI.

batch1Skipped.file-tree: removing hardcoded chevron aria-label regresses passing axe test; deferred fidelity item per RAC source

incidentalFindings.title: LinkPopover trigger nested inside Popover pressable → duplicate role=button name=Link (nested button)

reauditSummaries.summary: Real react-aria-components ToggleButton wrapper: genuine toggle semantics with onChange/data-selected output, consumer-driven Emoji/Count slots, and a readonly mode that cuts the onChange channel while preserving selected display — faithfully matches HeroUI Pro's 4 variant sections (default/disabled/read-only/sizes) with no fakes.

Built on real RAC/HeroUI engines (ColorPicker, ColorArea, ColorSlider, ColorSwatchPicker, ColorSwatch) with genuine controlled value, popover overlay, presets and area/slider drag — no fakes; the four contract tokens (popover-picker, presets, controlled-value, disabled-state) are all wired, with only minor gaps around disabled overlay propagation and a missing hex/format input.

Built on real react-aria-components NumberField (genuine increment/decrement, keyboard arrows/typing, min/max clamping, controlled+uncontrolled value) with @number-flow/react animated value display; all 13 documented variants are wired in the demo registry and all four contract tokens map to real RAC behavior.

Real engine work is present (controllable open hooks, cookie persistence, mod+b shortcut, Resizable panels, and a true Motion drag-to-close Sheet for the mobile aside), but the mobile sidebar has no overlay/sheet treatment and the icon-collapse rail is purely CSS-width with no real collapsed-rail interaction surface — the two biggest parity gaps vs HeroUI Pro.

Built on real react-aria primitives (ModalOverlay/Modal/Dialog + Autocomplete/useFilter + Menu/MenuSection) so command-dialog, grouped-results, shortcut-display, and live keyboard-search filtering are genuine; the only real gap is the global Cmd+K open shortcut, which is purely a decorative Kbd hint with no listener.

Real-engine wrapper: RAC Popover (cursor-anchored via a 0-size virtual anchor div) + HeroUI Menu, with genuine controlled/uncontrolled open, right-click/long-press/Shift+F10 triggers, submenus (SubmenuTrigger/SubmenuPopover), sections, separators, and selection passthrough — quality is high and the contract tokens are all backed by real interaction code; gaps are narrow.

Real react-aria-components ToggleButtonGroup/ToggleButton implementation with genuine FLIP slide indicator animation; single-select, controlled-value, and keyboard-navigation are all real (RAC-native) and test-covered, matching HeroUI Pro segment parity.

Real implementation built on a genuine engine: RAC Tree/TreeItem drives the menu (real roving-focus keyboard nav, typeahead, nested inline expand/collapse), the repo's Sheet (Drawer + RAC focus trap) powers mobile, and open state is properly controlled/uncontrolled with cookie persistence, a mod+b shortcut, and a navigate() hook — not a facade. Gaps are narrow: client-routing items lack real anchor semantics, and collapsed-icon nested menus have no flyout.

Plain semantic compound component (ol/li + context-driven slots, no RAC needed since HeroUI Pro Stepper is likewise presentational); real controlled/uncontrolled step state, both orientations, all sizes, derived step-state, native button steps when interactive, and all 19 variant sections wired in demos — no code-grounded parity gaps.
